 There are 2 main problems about why they think video games is bad. Firstly, they can be easily rage at all when they got killed by someone or lost a match of that game. Most of gamers nowadays are the kids, they can play their favorites game on their parent's laptop or their phone at every moment even when they eating or they can even play video games at the restroom. In general, they can play in every single place and the kids will get rage so easily that they can broke everything in the house just because one random guy killed the kid due to the first problem I'm talking about right now. Secondly, the video games is so attractive that they can easily got addicted, I've seen on YouTube about the kid who was addicted to a Shooting Game and he's crazy right now. I can see that game addiction is more affective than the drugs which is crazy. 
 However, some people do not think that video is wasting their time and bad as everyone see. Video games is the easiest way to learn a new language or you can learn something that you haven't know or heard before which is the most popular benefit. Video games is a good places to chill out with your friends and even your old friends, when you grew up, the video games is just a fun place to vibe, is not a try-hard game or took it seriously like you and your bros used to anymore. Now they only cares about chilling in-game and troll some people which is really really fun and have some unforgettable moments with them 
 In conclusion, while people thinking playing video game is goof off and suck, I believe that people should see the advantages and have a great look of the video games
feedback:
This essay attempts to discuss the two sides of playing video games, focusing on the negatives and positives. However, there are several areas for improvement to make the essay stronger and more coherent. 

Firstly, the introduction to the topic does not clearly restate the original prompt or provide a clear direction for the essay. It's essential to first present the topic accurately and then introduce personal opinions systematically within the body of the essay rather than in the introduction. Doing so will ensure that the essay follows a logical structure and meets the requirements of a balanced discussion.

The discussion of the negative aspects of video games could benefit from clearer and more specific examples. Instead of relying on a generalized anecdote from YouTube or vague references to gamers' behaviors, offering concrete studies or statistics could strengthen the argument. Additionally, the assertion that video game addiction is more impactful than drug addiction is a strong claim that requires substantial evidence to be convincing.

On the positive side, the mention of video games as a tool for learning and socializing is valuable. However, these points could be expanded with more detailed explanations or examples of how games have been directly beneficial in these areas. 

The conclusion of the essay does not effectively summarize the discussed points or present a clear personal stance on the issue. It's crucial for the conclusion to reflect on the content and provide a concise statement of the writer's view, based on the arguments presented.

Stylistic and grammatical issues also hinder the clarity and professionalism of the essay. Using correct subject-verb agreement, such as "Video games are" instead of "Video games is", and avoiding run-on sentences will improve readability. Proper punctuation and varied sentence structures can also enhance the essay's flow.

Overall, focusing on a clear structure, providing specific examples, and polishing grammar and style would significantly improve the essay. It's also important to clearly present and support personal opinions within the structure of the essay to effectively engage with the topic.

prompt: Staying at hotels when holidaying abroad is not the method to comprehend a nation and the people liv
essay:
To some, staying at hotels when holidaying abroad is not the method to comprehend a nation and the people living in it. From my point of view, I consider myself in agreement with the statement. 
 For a variety of reasons, it would be wrong to reckon that having an oversea holiday to learn about a country and its people is the best method. One of the main reasons is that hotels always offer tourists good and luxury services to confine them within the hotel zone to enjoy those services. It leads to the notion that quite a few tourists frequently be too lazy to discover the nation they are traveling just because of spending time on the services of the hotels. To make sense of that, for instance, quite a few hotels provide luxury meals and playing rooms to tourists so that they may take a whole day within the hotel zone. 
 Another reason is that doing so causes tourists to fail to obtain the knowledge they want in an oversea country. Therefore, people cannot realize the local culture or even what happens outside the zone of the hotels when they are in that zone. Quite a few harmful impacts may place pressure on local tourism because of transferring the wrong information about the country by tourists. To several tourists, sharing wrongly the knowledge they know is frequently happen, because sometimes they do not realize the knowledge they obtained is the case or not. 
 To conclude, my point is that if tourists want to comprehend more about a foreign country's culture and people, just go out of the comfortable zone and experience more so that they can know whatever they want. ( 281 words)
feedback:
The essay effectively addresses the topic, focusing on the viewpoint that staying at hotels during overseas holidays may not allow travelers to fully understand a country and its people. This clear stance is a strong point of the essay. However, there are several areas where improvements could significantly enhance the essay's clarity, coherence, and overall effectiveness.

Firstly, the introduction could benefit from a more detailed paraphrasing of the prompt, aiming for a fuller explanation over 3-5 sentences. For example, instead of immediately stating agreement with the statement, briefly describe the common practice of tourists staying in hotels and how this is perceived in relation to truly understanding a country. This approach sets a stronger foundation for the essay by clearly outlining the issue at hand.

Regarding the structure, the essay could be more impactful by ensuring each paragraph starts with a clear topic sentence that relates directly back to the thesis. This would help in guiding the reader through the argument in a more organized manner. Specifically, focus on two main reasons why hotel stays limit understanding of a country: 1) hotels offer luxury services that deter exploration, and 2) this confinement impacts genuine learning about the country. Each paragraph should explore these points in depth, directly linking them to the central thesis.

The essay also faces challenges with spelling, grammar, and style. For instance, "oversea" should be "overseas," and sentences could be simplified for clarity and coherence. Avoid overly complex phrases and aim for straightforward, precise language. This approach will make the essay more accessible and engaging to a broader audience.

Moreover, the argument would be strengthened by providing specific, real-world examples to support claims. While the essay mentions hotels offering luxury services, concrete examples of how this impacts tourists' understanding of local culture would be more persuasive.

Finally, the conclusion needs to effectively summarize the key points discussed, rather than introducing new arguments or repeating supporting reasons. A concise recap of the main reasons followed by a reaffirmation of the thesis can offer a strong closure, signaling to the reader that the essay has fully addressed the prompt.

In summary, focusing on these areas—paraphrasing the introduction, clarifying the structure with topic sentences, refining the language, providing concrete examples, and improving the conclusion—will greatly enhance the effectiveness and persuasiveness of the essay.

Furthermore, succeed is the only thing people fight for, the same as the caregivers, they want their youngsters to have the good position in society.  Many people believe this prespective lead to a positive development because ancientors have once said pressure can make a diamond. As a result, not only the child prouds of himself or herself but also he or she become the good example for many people. 
 In conclusion, as fas as I concern, chidren can be in their better version within the parents' pressure and this sould be encouraged more. I suggest parents should put pressure on their children in the right method so that advoided children being stressed.
feedback:
The essay you've written is on a topic that elicits strong opinions and you've chosen an interesting angle to approach it from. However, to enhance the clarity and effectiveness of your writing, there are several areas to consider for improvement.

However, it is crucial that the introduction focuses more on directly responding to the essay question without overly repeating the topic's wording. Instead of including your viewpoint in the introductory restatement, position it as a concise thesis statement that guides the reader through your arguments in the following paragraphs.

In terms of structure, your essay is divided into paragraphs that attempt to develop your reasoning. Nevertheless, the division between these paragraphs needs reconsideration. Your first reasoning paragraph effectively outlines the reasons parents might pressure their children, but your analysis on whether this leads to positive or negative development is placed in the conclusion rather than being explored in its own separate paragraph. This approach limits your essay's ability to fully develop and support the central argument. Remember, each major point you make should ideally be expanded upon in its own paragraph, especially when the essay prompt splits its inquiry into multiple parts. 

Regarding content, the use of examples and the effort to support your arguments are commendable. However, it's important to ensure that each argument receives sufficient development. The essay could benefit from a more balanced exploration of both the pros and cons of parental pressure, with each point backed by specific evidence or examples. 

Additionally, your essay concludes with a suggestion or solution, which, while thoughtful, diverges from the traditional conclusion approach. The conclusion should summarize the key points of your argument and reinforce your stance on the issue rather than introducing new ideas or proposals. A strong conclusion draws together your arguments in a way that reaffirms your thesis statement without simply repeating it verbatim.

On a linguistic level, there are several spelling, grammar, and stylistic issues to address:
- Be mindful of spelling errors and the correct use of words (e.g., "beginning" instead of "begining", "avoided" instead of "advoided").
- Pay attention to consistency in verb tenses and subject-verb agreement.
- Aim for clarity in your sentences. Sometimes simpler sentences can more effectively convey complex ideas.

In conclusion, paying attention to the organization of your essay, supporting your points with developed reasoning, and refining your grammar and style will significantly improve the clarity and persuasiveness of your writing. Remember, writing is a process that involves drafting and revising, so continue to practice and refine your approach.

prompt: The extinction of animal species such as land and marine animals results from human activities IELTS
essay:
Nowadays, the life of people has modrenized because of the great development of industrialization and technology. However, it is supposed that the extinction of animal species such as land and marine animals results from human activities, especially is deforestation and dynamite. This can stem from many causes, which calls for possible solutions to be adopted. 
 The extinction of animal species can be attributed to several factors. Firstly, one possible reason could be that deforestation is in alarming rate since the overpopulation booms. People have to meet the housing demand. Not only is the loss of animals' habitat threatened but also land animals will be hunted by wrongdoers who carry out the illegal activities. Another cause worth mentioning is that dynamite is the most cruelest way to catch marine animals. To be more specific, when using this weapon, there is an enormous explosion that makes penty of creatures injure seriously, even cause a thousand of deaths. Then they will be brought to sell. 
 There are several measures which can be taken to handle this issue. To begin with, should a heavy punishment be imposed to offenders by the government. This can be done by imprisoning them from three to five years for ruining the resources of environment to ensure they will not reoffend. In marine life, the government should prohibit people from catching fish by dangerous chemicals. Under any circumstances, if they want to catch fish mainly because having the end meet, their activities must be manual. 
 To a word, the extinction of animal species results from many causes, and there are several actions that can be taken to tackle this issue.
feedback:
The essay provided addresses the issue of animal species extinction due to human activities, specifically highlighting deforestation and the use of dynamite in fishing as primary causes. Furthermore, it suggests solutions, including imposing heavy punishments on offenders and regulating fishing methods. Here's how the essay could be improved:

1. **Response to the Topic**: The essay addresses the given topic by discussing the causes of animal species extinction and proposing solutions. Nevertheless, to enhance clarity and coherence, introducing the main causes and solutions concisely in the opening paragraph would set a clearer framework for the reader. This approach directly responds to the essay prompt and prepares the reader for the detailed discussion that follows.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, and Stylistic Issues**: There are a few areas that need attention. For example, "modrenized" should be corrected to "modernized," and "penty" should be corrected to "plenty." Additionally, phrases like "the most cruelest" are grammatically incorrect and redundant; it should be "the most cruel" or simply "the cruelest." To improve readability and ensure a professional tone, consider revising sentences that feel cumbersome or overly complex. Simplifying language will not only help in meeting the proficiency standards but also in making arguments more accessible to readers.

3. **Point of View Clarity**: The essay's point of view, advocating for actions against the causes of animal extinction, is generally clear. However, making a decisive stance in the introduction and using more assertive language throughout the essay could reinforce the author's viewpoint. For instance, instead of saying "This can stem from many causes," a stronger stance would be "Human activities, notably deforestation and the use of dynamite in fishing, are primary causes of this extinction."

4. **Arguments and Support**: The essay mentions causes and solutions but lacks detailed examples and transition sentences that could strengthen the argument. Using a structured approach with each paragraph containing a reason or solution followed by a supporting example, and then linking these with transition sentences, would significantly enhance the argumentative strength of the essay. For each cause or solution mentioned, providing specific, real-world instances or data would make the argument more convincing.

5. **Concluding Remarks**: The conclusion should not only restate the essay's main points but also synthesise them to reinforce your stance. An effective conclusion also indicates the significance of the solutions and the urgent need for action. Phrasing such as "In conclusion, addressing the root causes of animal species extinction through stringent regulations and public awareness is critical for preserving biodiversity" would offer a stronger, more persuasive closing statement.

By focusing on these areas—ensuring a direct response to the prompt, correcting linguistic errors, stating a clear viewpoint, structuring arguments effectively, and crafting a compelling conclusion—the essay will significantly improve in clarity, coherence, and persuasiveness.

prompt: Having time alone is the most ideal way to relax and relieve stress. Do you agree or disagree with the following statement? Use specific reasons and examples to support your answer.
essay:
the way people relax today 
 It has been controversial for a long time about how people can relax and relieve their stress. Some people believe having time with others have more advantages than being alone since we are a social being who prefer to interact with one another. I partially agree with the opinion in terms of several benefits it can bring us, but I believe that the most ideal way to relax and relieve stress is having time alone since all kinds of stress eventually stem from our mind. 
 Having time with others is meaningful activities to refresh ourselves by sharing our concerns and communicating with others. In general, people have common instinct desiring to talk or listen to someone so that it would be effective to have a relaxing time. Nonetheless this advantage, it also stresses people. According to a research, what stresses people in a house and job is a relationship with others. In other words, most of the people get stress as well as help from others. That is why it cannot be the ideal way to relax. 
 Having time alone will help you manage your mind. In contrast having time with others, having time alone will be helpful to control your mind since it will locate you separated from outer situation or concerns. It is really difficult to change an environment which you are facing with, however, you can change your mind easily. According to Viktor Frankl, who was a famous psychologist and imprisoned in Auschwitz, mentions that he did not have any physical freedom in prison but could totally control his mind by himself. As he emphasizes the importance of mind, only our mind determine if it would be stress or not. 
 Having time alone will change people fundamentally. There are some types of people who always complain whatever they do. Regardless of the result or process, they are accustomed to doing it. Although they just complain about this habit to others, it shortly releases their stress but it will not last for a long time. However, if the person has an enough time to reflect himself/herself by having time alone, they can change their mind. Changing their mind is not easy but it is possible by their own decision. Most of the people experience to do that while having time alone. 
 To sum up, having time alone is the ideal way to release and relax time rather than being with others because it is useful to manage your mind as well as change people fundamentally. In this regard, I think that being alone is the best option of relaxation
feedback:
This essay presents a clear point of view on the topic of relaxation, arguing that spending time alone is the most ideal way to relieve stress. However, there are areas in the essay that require improvement to meet the expectations of an English proficiency test essay format and content criteria.

1. **Response to Topic:** Your essay directly addresses the controversy surrounding people's preferences for relaxation. Your viewpoint is clear and strongly supported within the text. However, it's important that you directly incorporate personal experiences to support your arguments. English proficiency tests, especially the TOEFL, often look for personal anecdotes or references to popular culture rather than citations from research or studies. Sharing personal stories or observations can add depth to your essay and showcase your ability to use English in a nuanced and expressive manner.

2. **Essay Structure and Length:** For such tests, sticking to the prescribed essay structure is crucial. Typically, a five-paragraph format is expected, with each paragraph containing a maximum of five sentences. This structure helps in organizing arguments coherently and ensures clarity of thought. Your current essay surpasses this format, which may distract from its overall effectiveness. Consider restructuring your essay to comply with this format, focusing on concise and powerful statements within the set number of paragraphs and sentences.

3. **Spelling, Grammar, and Style:** There are minor spelling and grammatical errors throughout the essay which can detract from its overall clarity and persuasiveness. Paying close attention to verb tense, subject-verb agreement, and sentence structure can significantly improve the readability of your essay. Additionally, beginning the essay with a lowercase letter ('the way people relax today') can be seen as an oversight in formal writing; always capitalize the first word of a sentence. Moreover, varying your sentence structure and employing transitional phrases can enhance the flow and coherence of your narrative.

4. **Arguments and Point of View:** Your essay does well in presenting arguments supporting the idea that spending time alone is beneficial for relaxation and stress relief. However, the arguments would be stronger and more relatable with the inclusion of specific personal examples. For instance, briefly mentioning a personal moment of relaxation or a lesson learned during a solitary activity can vividly illustrate your points and make your essay more engaging.

In summary, your essay contains strong ideas and arguments about the topic of relaxation. The next steps for improvement include aligning with the required essay structure, more deeply integrating personal experiences, and refining the use of English for clearer and more effective communication. With these adjustments, your essay will not only adhere more closely to the expectations of English proficiency tests but also showcase a richer and more personal understanding of the topic.

prompt: Rate and comment on my argumentative essay for GRE- Late night television news viewership decline
essay:
The memorandum from the television station states that to increase viewership, it should restore the time devoted to weather and local news to its former level. The argument stated in the memorandum does not make for a cogent case and is rife with assumptions not backed by solid evidence. 
 First, nowhere in the memorandum, it is stated that since increasing time to national news and decreasing it to weather and local news, the viewership has decreased. The television station has received complaints from viewers regarding the coverage of weather and local news but it can not be inferred from this that some or many viewers stopped watching the late night program. 
 Also, sufficient conclusion about the number of complaints can not be drawn from statement issued by the television station. It could be just a handful or many but we don't know from the statement alone. Also, the content of complaints is also not very clear from the statement. Whether the complainants disliked the brevity of weather and local news or its quality, we can not be sure. Further survey among the late night program viewers is required to know for sure they are really unhappy about the change of duration of the TV programs. 
 Second, local businesses that cancelled the advertising contract with the television station did so because of increased time to national news, is not very clear from statement. The memorandum should have included statements from the local businesses themselves to arrive at this assumption. One can also not be sure of the number of contracts that were cancelled from this statement alone. 
 In conclusion, strong evidence is needed to support the conclusion stated in the memorandum that time devoted to weather and local news should be restored to its former level. As it stands, the argument does not make for a persuasive case. The television station must also verify, if at all there has been a viewership decline. Only when such evidence is achieved through viewer surveys and interviews from local businesses can the argument stated in the memorandum be strengthened.
feedback:
This essay demonstrates a significant improvement in analytical skills compared to previous work. The detailed examination of the television station's memorandum shows a deep understanding of the topic and an effective method of discussing each point. The arguments presented effectively question the original claims made in the memorandum, encouraging the reader to think critically about the issue. This is a strong aspect of the essay as it showcases the ability to engage in thoughtful analysis and to present a coherent critique.

However, there are some areas that could be improved to make the essay even stronger and more persuasive:

1. **Response to the Topic:** The essay successfully addresses the topic by analyzing the memorandum's argument critically. However, it would be beneficial to directly link each critique back to the question of how these oversights could affect viewership and the station's decision-making process.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, and Style:** There are a few grammatical inaccuracies that disrupt the flow of reading. Paying closer attention to sentence structure and the correct use of words will enhance clarity and readability. Simplifying complex sentences could also help in making the argument more accessible to all readers.

3. **Clarity of Author's Viewpoint:** The position against the memorandum's argument is clear throughout the essay. However, the critique could be strengthened by explicitly stating alternative actions or data that the television station could consider to make a more informed decision, thereby reinforcing the author's standpoint.

4. **Supporting Arguments:** The arguments are descriptive and question the memorandum's assumptions effectively. To further bolster these arguments, incorporating specific examples or potential data that could have supported the memorandum's claims would add depth. Suggesting concrete methods for how the station could better assess viewer preferences, such as through surveys or viewer feedback analysis, would also provide a more solid foundation for the critique.

In terms of overall improvement, focusing on refining grammatical skills and simplifying complex expressions will greatly enhance the persuasiveness and readability of future essays. By clarifying and expanding on supporting arguments with specific suggestions or data, the essay would not only critique but also offer constructive alternatives for the issues identified. This approach will create a more rounded analysis and showcase an advanced level of critical thinking and problem-solving skills. Keep up the good work, and continue to refine these skills for even better results in future essays.

prompt: Some students prefer to take a gap year between high school and university, to work or to travel.
essay:
In this day and age, instead of embarking on higher education, there is an increasing tendency for high school seniors to have a long-year break for working or traveling purposes. In this essay, both the merits and drawbacks of this trend will be discussed. 
 One of the most salient positives that deferring university enrollment can bring to the gap-year takers is the chance to enrich their life experiences and broaden their horizons. In other words, formal schooling just mainly focuses on academic background, which may lead to the lack of practical skills such as time management, negotiation skills, or interpersonal skills. Hence, entering the workforce or traveling to other cultures can afford students an opportunity to build and enhance these skills, which also gives them a competitive edge when pursuing college or working after their graduation. Another reason that taking a year off would be advantageous is that it would enable students to discover their hidden talents and strong points. As a result, it makes them easier to opt for a major in college and afterward identify a suitable career path. 
 However, there are multiple downsides associated with the gap-year option, one of which is the high risk of losing studying momentum. To be more specific, there is no denying that school leavers may consequently deteriorate their ability to perceive theoretical knowledge because they got used to the practical ones outside the college. Therefore, from the gap-year takers' perspective, pursuing college may become dull and be hard to resume. Furthermore, youngsters intending to take a year off, if do not have a clear plan of action, are likely to waste an enormous amount of valuable time and fall behind their friends. For example, they can spend an entire day on Netflix, Youtube, or other online platforms because they are disoriented with their journey whereas their old peers are expected to not only complete the first year at university but also develop either academic fields or practical skills through social clubs in college. 
 In conclusion, delaying tertiary education for one year to work or travel is a promising option that can change youngsters in an advantageous way; however, they need to have an efficient plan to avoid detrimental effects.
feedback:
The essay provides a balanced discussion on the merits and drawbacks of taking a gap year before entering higher education. However, for improvement, focusing on taking a clear stance regarding whether the advantages outweigh the disadvantages, or vice versa, would enhance the essay's effectiveness. 

1. **Response to the Topic:** The essay addresses the given topic by discussing both positive and negative aspects of taking a gap year before starting university. However, the essay would benefit from a clearer position or standpoint. It’s important to decide whether the positives outweigh the negatives or not and then structure the essay to support this viewpoint.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, and Style Issues:** Overall, the essay is well-written with minimal spelling or grammatical errors. Yet, to make the essay clearer, pay attention to sentence structure, ensuring each sentence is complete and doesn't contain more than one main idea. Simplifying complex sentences can also help in making your argument more accessible. Additionally, using transitional phrases can improve the flow from one argument to the next.

3. **Author’s Point of View:** The point of view is generally clear but could be strengthened by explicitly stating a personal opinion on the issue. After presenting both sides, it is essential to choose a side and argue why one set of points (either the advantages or disadvantages) is more significant.

4. **Arguments and Support:** The essay provides good insights into the benefits and drawbacks of taking a gap year. However, following a structured format for presenting these points could make the arguments more persuasive. Each benefit could be immediately contrasted with a potential downside, along with examples and explanations for why one outweighs the other. This structure helps in making a more compelling and organized argument.

For instance, when discussing the advantage of gaining life experience, immediately address a potential drawback (such as the loss of academic momentum), provide an example (e.g., struggling to adapt back to academic life), and explain why this disadvantage may outweigh the advantage or vice versa. This approach not only showcases an understanding of both sides of the argument but also demonstrates analytical thinking by evaluating which side is stronger based on the evidence provided.

In conclusion, while the essay effectively covers the topic, choosing a stance, improving clarity through sentence structure, and adopting a more analytical structure for presenting arguments would significantly enhance the overall quality and persuasiveness of the essay.

prompt: The tradition that family gets together to eat meals is disappearing. What are the reasons? impact?
essay:
Eating with families is the important custom of many areas in the world esspecially Asia .However , this tradition is becoming to disappear in the modern life due to a lot of reasons such as job and friends. Losing this tradition can lead to unhappy families , unrespect to the ancestor and others references. This essay will shed light on this problem. 
 First of all, job is one of the most common reasons .Nowadays, people are trying their best to improve their career by working overtime to get the higher profits or having dinner with partners to develop their relationship in order to co-operate in some contracts. Young generation is likely to spend their precious time on working in lieu of family ,they want to be rich and famous regardless of being unhappy and exhausted .Moreover, low wage and fail in some contracts that has an bad impact on them makes them discouraged and disappointed .That prevent them from coming home and they will find the other place to relax. 
 Second, having meals with family is boring. According to the research from Cambridge University , 90% of youngsters asked tend to eat with their friends and fellows because they are more interesting .Eating meals with older generation makes youngers feel bored and uncomfortable ,they have to care about their behaviors and their talk while having dinner with friends is more comfortable and fantastic. 
 On the other hand , family gets together to eat meals is a good tradition, it helps family's members feel happy and cozy so that it should be desevered. .Some groups of citizens realize that problem and they are trying to fix it. 
 In conclusion, if the tradition of eating meals with family is disappeared , it will have bad effects on the world and the ancestor will be disappointed about our generation.
feedback:
The essay focuses on the importance of eating with families and discusses modern challenges to this tradition. There are several areas for improvement to fully address the topic and enhance the essay's overall quality.

1. **Response to the Topic:** The essay does attempt to discuss the reasons for and impacts of families not eating together, which aligns with the original prompt. However, the discussion mixes multiple reasons in one paragraph and does not clearly separate the reasons from the impacts. It would be more effective to organize the essay into clearer sections—one for reasons and a distinct one for impacts.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, or Stylistic Issues:** There are several spelling and grammatical errors throughout the essay, such as "especially" misspelled as "esspecially" and incorrect punctuation placements like "However ,". Attention to detail in spelling, grammar, and punctuation is crucial. Also, the essay could benefit from more varied sentence structures to improve readability and flow.

3. **Point of View Clarity:** While the essay’s point of view is understandable, it could be made clearer by directly stating the personal stance on the tradition of family meals at the beginning and end of the essay. Expressing personal experiences or opinions would also make the argument more persuasive and relatable.

4. **Arguments and Support:** The arguments provided, such as jobs demanding too much time and meals with family being considered boring by some, are relevant. However, these arguments are somewhat superficial and would benefit from deeper analysis and support. Personal experiences or hypothetical examples could enrich this discussion. The mention of a study from Cambridge University stands out as an attempt to include evidence but remember, personal experience often makes for more compelling and achievable support in essays for English proficiency tests.

For future essays, consider structuring the body with one main reason and supporting details per paragraph, followed by a paragraph dedicated to the impacts. This organization will help keep your essay focused and make your arguments clearer to the reader.

In conclusion, summarizing the key points discussed in the body paragraphs and reiterating the main stance on the topic can strengthen the closing of the essay. This restatement helps unify the essay and ensures a strong, coherent finish.

Improving on these aspects will not only make the essay more aligned with the prompt but also enhance its overall clarity, persuasiveness, and effectiveness.

prompt: Some people think the government funding should not be used for supporting art and culture, others think supporting cultural activities may be beneficial for the population and the culture. Discuss both views and give your own opinion
essay:
Music and some art works are considered as the soul of human's life. Furthermore, these can be milestones of historical periods. That's the reason why government of many countries are investing amounts of money to preserve , and giving way to develop art and culture. Nevertheless, many people against this action. In my opinion, I believe that the advantages of aiding the cultural activities are far more outweighed than the disadvantages . 
 As we may know, culture of each country is different from the other. It partially contributes to the unique beauty of one country. However, the more modern the life is, the more risk many continents are facing with losing cultural identity. To prevent that matter, head of countries are putting their funds on supporting art and culture. For example, Ao dai which is a traditional dress of Vietnamese women are protected and inherited through many generations. Vietnamese Government has hold many international events where attendants are required to wear Ao Dai. In addition, most of the international show , especially having the participation of foreigners, are performed by artist dancers of Vietnam. These are some ways that Vietnamese government uses to reserve the beauty of Vietnamese traditions and help their women become more elegant and beautiful. Therefore, I strongly agree that supporting money for developing culture is the right things to do. 
 On the other hand, there are still many opinions dissapproving of raising money for cultural activities. The result of one survey shows that, people in this group believe that government funding should be spending on boosting the economy. However, From what I learned from school if one country want to be stronger , its society has to be stable first. To do that thing, culture is the most important factor. It teach people about behavior and the connection between men and men. 
 Besides, lots of people lost their trust with many organizations who take advantage of the money supporting for cultural. For example, they ask for help from publication and then they take away the large of money to do their own business. Government should have more methods to ensure the money comes directly to cultural cooperations . 
 In conclusion, reserving the beauty of culture is very important. Not only Government but also population should involve more to protect cultural heritage.
feedback:
The essay responds to the topic by discussing the importance of preserving culture through government funding but does so in a way that doesn't fully adhere to the prompt's requirements. Here are specific points of improvement:

1. **Response to Prompt**: The introduction attempts to paraphrase the prompt but deviates from what's asked by introducing an opinion prematurely and not clearly setting up a discussion of both views. It's essential to accurately paraphrase the prompt to show understanding. A better approach would be to state the topic and mention that both views will be discussed before presenting your opinion.

2. **Structure and Cohesion**: There's a lack of clear structure and transition between paragraphs. Each paragraph should ideally focus on a single idea with clear, concise sentences. The essay exceeds the typical limit of five sentences per paragraph without adding clarity. Aim for shorter paragraphs that directly address the topic. Additionally, the essay would benefit from clearer transitions to guide the reader through the argument smoothly.

3. **Clarity of Opinion**: While your opinion is presented, it's not done in the most effective manner. The task asks for a discussion of both views with your opinion to follow. Your opinion should be a clear, stand-alone statement based on the arguments discussed, rather than stating agreement or disagreement to an extent. 

4. **Argument Development**: The arguments are relevant but need further development. While cultural preservation examples are given, the essay lacks a thorough examination of why government funding in culture and art is preferable or necessary compared to other needs. Including more detailed reasoning and examples would strengthen these arguments.

5. **Grammar and Style**: There are some grammatical and stylistic issues that interrupt the flow of reading, such as spelling mistakes ("dissapproving"), grammar errors ("are facing with losing"), and awkward phrasing. Simplifying sentences, correcting verb tenses, and ensuring subject-verb agreement can resolve these. 

6. **Conclusion**: The conclusion should succinctly summarize the discussion and restate the thesis and your opinion. Avoid introducing new information in the conclusion, as it should wrap up the essay's arguments. The current ending feels open-ended and lacks a decisive summary of your viewpoint and the arguments supporting it.

Improving these aspects can enhance the essay's clarity, coherence, and effectiveness in responding to the prompt. Practice restructuring your essays to include clear, distinct sections (introduction, body paragraphs for each view, your opinion, and conclusion), and spend time refining grammar and style for better readability.

prompt: Nowadays people use bicycles less as a form of transport? Why is that? What can we do to encourage .
essay:
With the variety of vehicles in modern life, the number of bike users is remarkably dropping, which is one of the most head-aching issues in several countries. This essay aims to analyze the causes of this tendency and propose some plausible solutions. 
 It is undeniable that bicycles' loss of popularity is the inevitable consequence of various factors. Accordingly, very few people choose bikes as their form of transport because of the shortcomings of bicycles. More apparently, riding a bike requires time-consuming and physically demanding. It is not to mention the fact that bicycles do not enable people to carry heavy lifts or travel long distances. Thus, due to the improvement of living standards, most of them are likely to switch to motorized vehicles, which are fast and convenient. Furthermore, humans tend to lead a sedentary lifestyle. In particular, even in short distances, motorbikes and cars are chosen instead of bicycles. 
 Therefore, to encourage bike riders, the combination of the following resolutions is of utmost importance. Undoubtedly, the government launches propaganda campaigns to promulgate the benefits of riding a bicycle. Heightening public awareness of bike riding not only helps them fortify their physical health but also contributes to environmental protection. As result, bicycles gain a public attraction as a form of transports. Moreover, the governments invest in paths' construction exclusively for bike users. This solution aims to ensure the bikers' safety, which brings to them a reasonable reason for using bicycles for commuting or exercise. Stark examples of this could be seen in Netherlands and Germany, where the number of riders is highest in the world, well exemplify this. 
 In conclusion, the limitation of bicycles and abuse in motorized vehicles impact the popularity of bikes. Thus, propaganda campaigns and infrastructure of bike riders are necessary to stimulate it in normal life.
feedback:
Your essay provides an insightful analysis of why bike usage is declining and offers interesting solutions to counteract this trend. However, there are several areas where your essay could be improved to make your arguments more compelling and your writing clearer.

1. **Response to the Topic:** It seems your essay briefly addresses the prompt but lacks a direct restatement of it. It's essential to closely restate the prompt to ensure your essay is directly answering every part of the question. Remember, the introduction should outline what you will discuss without adding new information that wasn't presented in the prompt. Aim to directly respond to the question asked with a clear outline of your response in the introduction.

2. **Clarity and Cohesion:** Your essay would benefit from more concise and informative sentences, especially in the body paragraphs. Each paragraph should convey a clear idea within five sentences. Try to remove unnecessary introductory sentences that don't add value to your argument. This will help make your essay more coherent and easy to understand. Your goal should be to present your ideas in a way that makes them easy to read and comprehend at a glance.

3. **Spelling, Grammar, and Style:** While your essay is generally understandable, there are minor spelling and grammar errors. Paying closer attention to these details will improve the overall quality of your writing. Consider using simple and clear language to express your ideas, which will make your essay more accessible to a wide audience, including those who are marking your work.

4. **Argument Support:** You've made an effort to support your arguments with examples and reasoning. However, the connection between your points and examples like those from the Netherlands and Germany could be strengthened. Clarify how these examples support your argument to make your position clearer to the reader.

5. **Point of View:** Your essay does a decent job of presenting your point of view, but at times it gets lost in the lengthy explanations. Try to assert your perspective more clearly by stating your main points directly and supporting them with concise arguments.

In conclusion, focus on directly answering the prompt with clear and concise sentences. Avoid unnecessary introductions, ensure your paragraphs are informative and to the point, and careful proofreading will help eliminate minor errors. Use examples more effectively to support your arguments, and strive to make your point of view unmistakable.

prompt: All youngsters should do volunteer work in their leisure time to support the community?
essay:
Young people are suggested to be involved in community work in their spare time as a chance to raise hands to help the local area. From my point of view, it certainly makes sense for youngster's engagement in some volunteering tasks with specific reasons below. 
 From the first standpoint, not only can young people contribute their aids to arduous class, but they also reap a big number of benefits from doing unpaid work, especially strengthening their connectivity between them and society. Only by doing volunteering could the young further their experiences in their future jobs, considering this as a practicing chance. Better still, volunteering can stand out their profile application and the percentage of getting hired is higher. Through these meaningful works, youngsters are likely to count their blessings and get contentedness in life as well as self-satisfaction with their contribution to society. 
 Moreover, on the invaluable side of the community's donations, these benefactions contribute not a small development to the general life. There are numerous unprivileged people so being volunteered will lessen their infelicitous lives. The higher the social safety net is, the more prosperous the country will be, this term has emphasized the direct proportion between volunteering and society. In addition, young people can cultivate their minds and easily to be responsible citizens by way of helping miserable walks of life. 
 In conclusion, I strongly cling to my opinion that youngsters should contribution their efforts to volunteer work because it allows them to get a lot of advantages and have a well-rounded perspective of life.
feedback:
The essay effectively addresses the topic, which is commendable. You've made a clear argument in favor of young people engaging in community service during their free time. This shows a good understanding of the essay prompt.

However, there are a few areas for improvement to make your essay stronger and more compelling.

1. **Clarity on Your Stance**: While you express agreement with the idea of young people volunteering, the strength of your agreement could be more explicitly stated. Mentioning your strong agreement at the beginning and restating it in a slightly different way at the end will make your position clearer and more persuasive to the reader.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, and Stylistic Issues**: There are a few grammatical and stylistic issues that need attention. For instance, the apostrophe in "youngster's engagement" mistakenly suggests possession; it should be "youngsters' engagement" to indicate plurality. Additionally, some phrases like "contribute their aids to arduous class" are awkwardly constructed and could be made clearer. Simplifying complex phrases will help in making your arguments more accessible.

3. **Point of View**: Your point of view is noticeable throughout the essay, which is good. It’s evident that you favor young people participating in community service. To enhance this further, consider providing personal examples or more detailed explanations for why you hold this opinion. This adds depth and personal touch to your essay.

4. **Arguments and Supporting Details**: You've done well in providing arguments to support your viewpoint, particularly highlighting the benefits for both the volunteers and the community. To strengthen your essay, consider adding more specific examples or evidence. For example, mentioning a particular volunteering activity and how it directly benefits both the individual and community could make your argument more convincing.

5. **Cohesion and Coherence**: Your essay has a good flow, and your ideas transition smoothly from one to the next. This is a significant strength. To improve, watch out for overcomplicated sentences and consider breaking them into simpler, clearer statements. This will help in maintaining the reader's attention and ensuring your points are well understood.

In summary, your essay is on the right track with a clear stance and relevant arguments. Paying attention to the clarity of your position, simplifying your language for clearer communication, and enhancing your supporting arguments with specific examples will elevate the quality of your essay significantly. Keep up the good work, and keep practicing your writing skills.

prompt: IF YOU NEED TO SELECT BETWEEN BOOK AND E-BOOK, WHICH ONE IS BETTER?
essay:
Many people argue that, instead of using books that are made from paper, it is more convenient for people in the world to spend money on using electronic book whose content is displayed on the screen of tablet or smartphone. In my opinions, I totally agree with this idea. 
 The first reason is that if people use the electronic book, they could spend less money than using the paper book. Thanks to the e-book, people are able to download the content from the website, so that the cost of paper does not add-in the total cost of the book making the book more affordable. In addition, there is a mutual benefit for both end users and publishers if they use the electronic-book. For example, when users choose electronic-book, the cost of material to produce book and the cost of delivery can be eliminated, as a result, the book is cheaper. Not only so, e-book can be paid by an online transaction which means user transfer money through credit card instead of monetary payment. 
 Finally, reading electronic-book can protect the environment. Because the paper is made of wood, which means publishers have to cut down many trees in the forest. Deforestation could cause climate change. Nowadays, many protection forest areas are cleared by illegal forest users, illegal logging or forest clearance to produce paper lead to no natural forest for the wild animal to live. In addition, establishers must use coal or oil to operate the paper manufacturing system, which potentially pollutes environment. 
 In conclusion, it is convenient for people to read electronic-book instead of using the printed book/ real book. Using electronic book helps people not only save money, but also protect the forest. The government should encourage people to use e-book by imposing taxes on paper books.
feedback:
Your essay addresses an important topic: the preference for electronic books over traditional paper books. Your argument in favor of electronic books is clear and supported by several reasons, which makes your point of view understandable. Here are some specific points to consider for improvement:

1. **Topic Response & Cohesion**: Your essay provides a clear stance supporting the use of electronic books for both economic and environmental benefits. However, ensure every point or reason you introduce is fully explained. For instance, when discussing the benefit of online transactions in purchasing e-books, expanding on how this method is more efficient or secure compared to traditional purchasing methods would strengthen your argument and maintain cohesion within your paragraph.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, and Style**: You've done well in avoiding major spelling mistakes, but there are areas to improve in grammar and style. For example, avoid starting sentences with the word "because" as it's more commonly used to connect ideas within a sentence rather than to open a new sentence. This will enhance the grammatical accuracy of your essay. Also, strive to maintain a formal academic tone by not using slashes (e.g., printed book/real book). Instead, choose one term or use synonyms without a slash to maintain formality.

3. **Clear and Descriptive Arguments**: Your arguments are clear, particularly where you point out the cost-effectiveness of e-books and their environmental benefits. To improve, ensure that each argument is accompanied by detailed examples or explanations. For instance, when discussing the environmental benefits, including specific statistics or studies could further reinforce your point. 

4. **Avoiding Informal Expressions and Improving Formality**: Remember, an academic essay should maintain a formal tone throughout. Phrases like "In my opinions," could be more formally expressed as "In my opinion," since 'opinion' typically does not need to be pluralized in this context. Also, consider replacing informal phrases with more formal alternatives where appropriate.

In conclusion, your essay’s stance is commendable, and your efforts to argue in favor of electronic books are clear. To elevate your essay, focus on expanding your explanations where needed, improving grammatical accuracy, and adhering to a more formal style of writing. These modifications will not only enhance the clarity of your arguments but also align your essay more closely with academic writing standards. Keep up the good work, and with these adjustments, your essays will continue to improve.

prompt: Some people believe that it is good to share as much information as possible in scientific research, business and the academic world. Others believe that some information is too important or too valuable to be shared freely. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
essay:
Information has been a key issue nowadays due to its power. The one who keeps in hand the largest amount is eventually considered to be the most powerful. This leads to a debatable question: whether we should spread freely all the information or keep it in private for the sake of peace and power. In this essay, I will try my best to give a general view of both sides. 
 These days, people are claiming that sharing information creates educational communities. Living anywhere, global citizens can learn from others and can give away what they know. Balancing the world of information, this stimulates creativity and competitiveness, especially among the youths. Young people will then create new ideas, keeping up and enhancing the innovation. This, undoubtedly, leads to a better world. After all, everyone deserves to be aware of what is going on around them. Huge impact can come from the smallest pack of data. 
 On the other hand, spreading information freely can lead to potential danger. Indeed, those who have anti - ethical purposes can also learn the information and utilize it to fulfil their own aim. For example, terrorists are the ones we should keep an eye on as the threats they make may result in great loss, which we have already witnessed from the past. Thus, this explains partly why there are opinions stating that some information is too important or too valuable to be shared freely. The less information can reach those who have such intention, the safer, they believe, the world is. 
 To conclude, debatable as it seems, the question can not be answered in the exact right way. However, in my opinion, I would take the side and say that information should be shared freely. I believe in the rapid progress humanity will make if this move is set, and dangers can slowly be pushed away.
feedback:
The essay successfully addresses the topic by discussing the importance of information in today's society and whether it should be freely shared or kept private. The argument is well-structured, presenting both sides of the debate before concluding with a personal stance. However, there are areas where improvement is needed to enhance clarity, coherence, and the effectiveness of the argument.

1. **Avoid Assumptions and Rhetorical Questions:** The introduction starts with a broad assumption about information and power. It’s better to present clear, factual statements to introduce your topic. Additionally, using rhetorical questions can sometimes make the essay seem less original. Instead, directly state your points to keep the argument clear and strong.

2. **Use of Topic Sentences:** Each paragraph should start with a clear topic sentence that introduces the main idea of the paragraph. This helps the reader understand the key point right away. For example, rather than starting with filler words or broad statements, directly state "Sharing information promotes educational growth" or "Restricting information access can prevent misuse."

3. **Balance Between Public Discussion and Personal Opinion:** While it’s excellent to explore different viewpoints, remember to clearly include your own perspective as part of the main body of the essay, not just in the conclusion. This means dedicating a separate paragraph to fully explain and justify your personal stance. Doing so will make your argument more compelling and balanced.

4. **Word Count and Coherence:** Aim for concise, coherent paragraphs with 3-5 sentences each to stay within a reasonable word count and ensure clarity. Overwriting by including too many details can distract from your main points. Focus on being specific and straightforward in each paragraph.

5. **Grammar and Style:** There are minor spelling, grammar, and stylistic issues that need attention, such as the correct use of hyphens (e.g., "anti-ethical") and consistency in terms such as "information" (avoid switching to "data" unless specifically referring to data). Improving these will enhance the readability of your essay.

By addressing these points, your essay will become more focused, persuasive, and easier to follow, showcasing a clear and well-justified viewpoint on a complex issue.

prompt: Living in large urban areas has turned into the most attractive choice of many. It may be an issue.
essay:
Living in large urban areas has turned into the most attractive choice of many. While inhabiting major cities offer a multitude of opportunities, mega-city dwellers face a large number of issues on every day of their lives. This essay will examine some of these problems and then propose some possible solutions. 
 To begin with, traffic congestion is a serious issue which everyone living in a big city has been struggling with. The increasing number of personal vehicles in addition to the low cost of fuels has led to bumper-to bumper traffic and gridlocks in these cities. To address this problem, governments should provide not only more frequent but also high quality public transportation. Furthermore, more publicity campaign must be done to encourage using bikes and other not-motorist and clean modes of transportation. 
 Another major issue, is high price of property. For many, buying a small house in such cities is a dream that can never be fulfilled. Statistics show that more that 50% of a city-inhabitant is spent on accommodation. Take Hong Kong as an example, to rent s one meter square accommodation, more than 200 us dollars is charged. To solve this, governments should put more resources into affordable housing and they should provide more subsidies to lessen the burdens of buying a decent house for people with low financial capabilities. 
 To conclude, dwelling in a major city, considering its many benefits they bring about, is full of different issue, which cannot be fully addressed. The government should start tackling these issues such as traffic congestion and the high costs of accommodation, otherwise living in large cities would be the last choice one might make.
feedback:
This essay successfully identifies two significant urban challenges: traffic congestion and high property prices. However, there are several areas where it could be improved to more effectively respond to the topic, enhance clarity and coherence, and correct grammatical and stylistic errors.

Firstly, addressing the essay's response to the topic, it’s crucial to directly answer the questions posed by the essay prompt. The prompt appears to inquire about significant problems of living in large urban areas and possible solutions. While the essay identifies problems and proposes solutions, it could more robustly match the structure suggested by the prompt. Specifically, the essay should start with a clearer thesis statement that outlines the major points of discussion—both the problems and their corresponding solutions.

Regarding clarity and sentence structure, opt for simpler and more precise language to convey your points. For example, instead of saying "a large number of issues," it's clearer and more direct to say "many issues." Also, pay attention to small but important grammatical details, such as adding hyphens to compound adjectives (e.g., "bumper-to-bumper traffic" and "one-meter-square accommodation"). These adjustments not only help with the readability of the essay but also demonstrate a higher level of English proficiency.

The essay occasionally struggles with coherence and unity. For example, it discusses traffic congestion and high property prices as separate entities without illustrating a clear connection between them. A more cohesive approach would be to discuss these issues within a unified framework, showing how they might relate or contribute to one another, and then proposing integrated solutions. Moreover, solutions should not solely rely on government intervention; considering the personal or community level actions can also provide a more rounded perspective on addressing the problems.

Additionally, the essay’s structure could be improved. The recommendation to include both issues and their solutions within the same paragraph(s) for enhanced cohesion is noteworthy. Also, ensure that the conclusion effectively summarizes the key points discussed without introducing new ideas or continuing the argument. The conclusion should leave the reader with a clear understanding of the essay's main arguments and the proposed solutions.

Finally, while the essay does well to provide specific examples, such as referencing property prices in Hong Kong, it could benefit from more detailed explanation or additional examples to support its arguments further. 

By addressing these points, the essay’s clarity, coherence, and engagement with the topic would be significantly enhanced, providing a more compelling and comprehensive response to the essay prompt.

prompt: In many countries, very few young people read newspapers or follow the news on TV
essay:
In many countries, very few young people read newspapers or follow the news on TV.What do you think are the causes of this?What solutions can you suggest? 
 There is a little likelihood that the teenager will keen to chase the news from newspapers or TV. As the development of Internet greater than before,youngster bombard by the enormous information from different websites. The purpose of the technology is offering people tools to enhance the progression of human society. When it comes to the changing of receving news, teenagers is the group that acceptance is higher than elderly. 
 Although the influence of information is a crucial element to stimulate the young generation, the life style of getting news has transform into creative ways. Newspapers and TV programs are not the only resource to catch the latest news. Instead,the Internet have supersede the traditional news transportation and the improvement of the high-technology replace the old-fashion one. Indeed,the impact of technology is the main reason to eliminate the needs of newspapers and other traditional gadgets which is the important way to transmit information before the thriving of Internet. As a matter of fact, the young people diminish the purchasing rate on newspapers and turn into website reading. 
 By and large, young generation prefer Internet rather than old-fashion ways to accept the data. As the nature youth, they tend to utilize the new products. In order to receive latest news, they choose to the fastest way to get the knowledge they need. 
 Anyway,the main purpose of TV and newspapers is spreading information,there is no need to change the youngster life style on Internet. In my personal point of view, the progression of transporting news will be replaced by other innovation of tools. It is an inevitable trend in modern society, and the only solution should be under the supervision of public. However,the future of human is unpredictable,and only a few can stop the transformation of life style.
feedback:
The essay addresses the topic of why fewer young people are engaging with newspapers and TV for their news, shifting instead towards the internet. However, there are several ways in which it could be improved to meet the requirements of an English proficiency test essay, specifically like that of the IELTS Task 2 essay format.

1. **Response to the Topic**: The essay attempts to discuss the causes of a shift in how news is consumed by younger generations and suggests that technological advancements are the main reason. However, the essay lacks a clear introduction that paraphrases the prompt and outlines what the essay will discuss. It’s important to start with a clear paraphrase of the question. For example, you could begin with: "There’s a noticeable decrease in the number of young people looking for news in traditional forms like newspapers and television. This essay aims to explore the reasons behind this trend and suggest possible solutions."

2. **Structure and Clarity**: The essay structure does not follow the recommended format, which usually consists of an introduction, 2-3 body paragraphs each discussing a unique point with reasons and examples, followed by a conclusion. Each body paragraph should start with a clear topic sentence that introduces the main idea of the paragraph. Try to diversify your arguments rather than repeating the same point. This will not only make your essay stronger but also showcase your ability to reason and analyze different aspects of a topic.

3. **Spelling, Grammar, and Stylistic Issues**: There are several grammatical errors and awkward phrasings. For instance, "There is a little likelihood that the teenager will keen to chase the news" could be rephrased to "It is unlikely that teenagers will be keen to follow the news." Pay attention to article use ("the Internet" not "Internet"), subject-verb agreement ("the Internet has superseded" not "have supersede"), and consistent tense usage. Ensuring correct grammar and varied sentence structures will enhance the readability and professionalism of your essay.

4. **Point of View Clarity**: Your essay tentatively presents a viewpoint but gets muddled with generalized statements. When expressing a point of view, especially if the prompt doesn’t explicitly ask for it, ensure it is backed up with clear, logical arguments and examples. It’s also crucial to conclude your essay by summarizing your discussion succinctly rather than introducing new ideas or continuing the argument. 

5. **Argument Support**: While you mention the impact of technology as a root cause, your essay would benefit from more detailed examples and reasons to support your claims. Discussing alternative news sources and their appeal to the youth, comparing them with traditional media, and suggesting specific solutions to engage the younger generation with diverse news formats could strengthen your essay.

To improve, focus on clearly structuring your essay with a defined introduction, body paragraphs, and a conclusion. Work on diversifying your points, ensuring grammatical accuracy, and directly addressing the essay prompt with clear arguments and examples. Revising these areas will significantly enhance the quality of your essay and better align it with the requirements of English proficiency tests.

prompt: IELTS - Writing task 2 - Allow children to make their own choices will turn them into selfish person
essay:
Parenting has long been a major topic regarding both scholarly research and daily discussion for decades. Despite the fact that there is an increasingly popular opinion claiming that letting children have a complete decision on daily routines such as meal, styling, and amusement will eventually trigger negative individualism, other people are prone to believe that independent decision making is crucial for nurturing children more comprehensively. 
 As a matter of fact, decision making is largely considered a compulsory skill regardless of various personality tendencies and thinking patterns. And not to mention that decision making is still a difficult task for grown-up individuals, it is becoming more and more significant to teach children how to make decisions on their own for the ultimate purpose of achieving self-sufficient and self-dependent at later ages. Indeed, the most optimal approach to illustrate to children the nature of decision making is to initially imply it in their simple daily routines. In other words, the exposure of children toward the choices of what to eat, what to wear, and what to play will concretely form a solid foundation for them to retain consistency in further life-long issues which requisite decisive characteristics. 
 On top of that, individuals who constantly put themselves first and only care about themselves while ignoring the well-being of others are more likely a result of failure in parenting regarding numerous aspects from an early age, rather than being affected by the opportunities to conduct choices that are passed on by their parents. Common belief indicates that children are a real reflection of their parents and the environment in which they grew up in. For instance, if a mother or father has a frequent tendency in behaving selfishly toward the surrounding people, there would be a significant likelihood that a child will grow up being selfish and lack sympathy as well as possess a low level of emotional intelligence. Nurturing patterns and influences from parents' behaviors are the key drivers that contribute greatly to a children's personality and attitude toward the surrounding environment in the future, rather than the early decision-making opportunities that are held accountable for the mentioned controversy. 
 According to the above analysis, I incline to believe it should not be certain that children's decision making on simple routines will bring along negative individualism in the upcoming ages. Alternatively, encouraging independent decision making will surprisingly cater for a more comprehensive outcome regarding self-sufficient and self-dependent.
feedback:
The essay discusses the topic of parenting and decision-making by children, presenting a clear stance in favor of allowing children to make small decisions to foster independence and self-reliance. However, there are several areas where improvement is needed to enhance the essay's effectiveness and adhere to essay-writing best practices, especially for English proficiency tests.

1. **Adherence to the Topic and Word Count:**
   - The essay should focus closely on the prompt provided, without including extraneous information. The initial sentence, for example, could be perceived as not directly related to the core argument, which might lead to deductions in score for deviating from the topic.
   - The essay exceeds the recommended 300-word count significantly. Being concise is crucial in essay writing, especially under timed conditions. Practice writing with a timer to develop a sense of how to express your ideas within the word limit.

2. **Presentation of Arguments:**
   - While the essay does navigate the discussion around children's decision-making, it primarily offers a singular perspective without adequately exploring or contrasting different viewpoints. For proficiency tests, it's beneficial to discuss various perspectives before concluding with your own stance. This approach shows critical thinking and a comprehensive understanding of the subject.
   - Incorporating comparative viewpoints with phrases like "some people" versus "other people," followed by a public vs. personal analysis for each, would enrich the essay by showing a balanced consideration of the topic.

3. **Clarity of the Author’s Point of View and Conclusion:**
   - The essay ends on a personal note, aligning with the idea of fostering decision-making in children but lacks a fully developed conclusion. A conclusory paragraph summarizing the main points and restating the author's position clearly would provide a strong finish. Avoid leaving the essay open-ended to ensure the reader is left with a clear understanding of your stance.

4. **Stylistic and Language Considerations:**
   - Be mindful of sentence structure and verbosity. Shorter, well-structured sentences can make your argument more accessible and straightforward, especially for a diverse audience or in a test scenario.
   - Pay attention to spelling, grammar, and punctuation. Errors in these areas can distract from the content and impact the overall professionalism and readability of your essay.

5. **Specific Recommendations for Improvement:**
   - Start by refining the introduction to directly address the essay prompt.
   - Limit the use of overly complex vocabulary that might make the essay less clear or more difficult to follow.
   - In the body paragraphs, include contrasting viewpoints and analyze them before presenting your conclusion.
   - End with a summarizing paragraph that encapsulates the essence of your argument, reinforcing your stance on the topic.

By addressing these points, the essay will be better structured, more persuasive, and more effectively aligned with English proficiency test expectations.

prompt: Exercise or a balanced diet to health? Discussion essay.
essay:
It is commonly believed that the way toward being healthy is by doing the workout. Which leads to others consider that a regular balanced eating habit is far more crucial. In this essay, I will discuss both points of view and present my opinion to this discussion below. 
 On the one hand, some think that the most effective way to health is by doing exercise. In other words, exercise helps one's body consume the food and convert them into energy as well as expel the toxins by sweating it out. As a result, it keeps one body's system working properly while kicking toxins out of one's body. 
 On the other hand, others believe that it is far more important to have a right proportion of eating habit. Particularly, having a balanced diet would avoid the negative effect of intaking exceeded one particular substance from the food and therefore, it would keep one's body stay healthy by getting all the good nutrients from a wide variety of food. 
 Finally, I consider that doing exercise regularly and keeping a healthy diet is equally important. Specifically, by only exercising without giving up on junk food or overly consumed sugar or salt in food would eventually lead to diabetes. Nevertheless, merely regulating the eating habit without a structural and consistent workout schedule would easily suffer any form of heart diseases as its heart muscles are not strong enough to withstand the sudden pressure that might occur in everyday life. 
 In conclusion, while exercise could help a body function properly. A healthy diet could avoid consuming one specific substance in food excessively. And I suggest that they are both crucial in health because without either one of its habits, which would potentially induce some serious diseases to a person.
feedback:
The essay provides a thoughtful discussion on the importance of both exercise and a balanced diet for maintaining health, which closely aligns with the topic. Each paragraph focuses on a single aspect of the debate, followed by a personal viewpoint that integrates these aspects effectively. This structure showcases an ability to analyze and present arguments clearly, indicating strong potential in essay writing.

However, the introduction could be improved for clarity. The opening statement presents the topic somewhat confusingly, which might make it hard for readers to immediately grasp the essay's focus. Consider rephrasing it for better clarity, similar to: "A common belief is that exercise is the sole path to perfect health. However, the importance of a balanced diet is increasingly recognized as vital for one's overall health. This essay will explore both viewpoints and offer a personal perspective." This approach sets a clearer stage for the discussion.

There are some areas where grammar, word choice, and sentence structure could be refined to enhance readability and coherence. For instance:
- The phrase "doing the workout" could be simplified to "exercising".
- Instead of "others consider that a regular balanced eating habit", consider "others believe that maintaining a balanced diet".
- Use "one's body" consistently instead of switching between "one body's system" and "one's body".

Your conclusion successfully summarizes the essay's main points, underscoring the importance of both exercise and diet. However, it might be strengthened by directly relating back to the discussion, emphasizing how both are equally essential and interdependent for a healthy lifestyle.

Remember, a strong essay is not only about presenting your ideas but also about how clearly and effectively you communicate them. Paying attention to clarity from the start, using precise language, and ensuring grammatical correctness will make your arguments more persuasive and your writing more impactful.
