Keep practicing, as your ability to discuss complex ideas is evident and will continue to improve with attention to these aspects.

prompt: Being dependend in the modern world
essay:
Nowadays, public opinion is divided on whether we, as modern citizens, are becoming more or less dependent on others. As far as I am concerned, living in a technologically advanced society, in some way or another, forces us to increasingly depend on each other. 
 The primary reason why it could be believed that we are having more independence than people in the past is the development of democracy and civil rights. People are now given more freedom to make contributions to building public policies and to make personal decisions. Today's couples, for instance, may freely choose their spouses without receiving approval from their families or relatives. In addition, along with the wide spread of modern lifestyle, people are more likely to place a higher priority on individualism than on collectivism, which means that they tend to be taught to solve their problems by themselves instead of seeking helps from surrounding people. 
 Nevertheless, I would be in agreement with advocates of the idea that people are developing a deep dependence on each other these days. Firstly, it is the growing complexity of social division of labor that makes individuals more aware of others' important roles in their lives. For example, both parents who have full-time jobs would not pay full attention to their work if there was not the support from babysitters, housekeepers and so on. Secondly, just as ancient squares, social media is viewed as an ideal environment where people can more easily keep in touch and contact with one another than ever before despite geographic and time barriers. 
 In conclusion, while there are some opposing viewpoints on the issue, I still believe that we are living in a profoundly interdependent world. ( 282 words)
feedback:
The essay effectively addresses the topic by discussing whether modern citizens are becoming more or less dependent on others, offering views on both sides of the argument. However, the structure of the essay can be improved to enhance clarity and impact. Presently, the essay attempts to present opposing viewpoints alongside the author's perspective, but it could be more effective by distinctly separating these elements. 

A clear way to organize your thoughts is through the use of either a three-paragraph or a two-paragraph approach. The three-paragraph method involves discussing the two contrasting public opinions in their own paragraphs and then presenting your personal viewpoint in a third paragraph. This structure makes it easier for readers to understand the different perspectives before considering your stance. Alternatively, the two-paragraph approach integrates the comparison of public opinions with your personal viewpoint within the same paragraphs. This method directly shows how your understanding of the issue compares to the general public opinions, potentially providing a more nuanced discussion.

The essay could benefit from a more disciplined approach to spelling, grammar, and style. Watch out for sentences that could be clearer or more concise. Additionally, ensuring that each argument is well-supported with examples or evidence will strengthen the essay's persuasiveness.

The point of view is understandable; you believe that technology and social complexities have increased our dependence on one another. To improve clarity, explicitly stating this thesis at the beginning could help guide the reader through your arguments.

Overall, the essay presents a thoughtful viewpoint. However, by refining the structure, enhancing clarity around the arguments, and addressing minor language issues, the essay could present a more compelling and polished argument. Aim to lead the reader through a well-organized discussion that clearly juxtaposes public opinions with your insights, thereby offering a rich analysis of the topic at hand.

prompt: Emotion based decisions will have high tendency to be wrong - they should be taken with free mind
essay:
I agree with author's observation here. Emotion based decisions will have high tendency to be wrong as compared to the decisions taken with free mind or irrespective of any emotions. And later justifying these decisions with logic remains just as a formality. 
 Emotions will bias a persons thought process. Focus will be more on the emotinally supported points and their benefits. Focus on negativity of the same view will be lost. For reference if I want to make a decison on fighting between two kids. One of them is my brother. I I'll think emotionally it has high propensity for me to be in favour of my brother. Cause emotions will make my brothers point of view more strong and important. I'll not be able to see what wrong he did. It's very much important to see the both positive nad negative sides before making a decision. 
 It'll also eclipse the facts to which emotions doesn't support. Here we'll be missing the good or positive side of the emotionally unsupported view. It will result in partial thinking or a very restricted thinking. Same way here if I take the previously mentioned example in this scenario, other kid's faults will seems to me very bad but his innocuousness I'll not be able to see those will be eclipsed by the emotional state. 
 In both of the cases I'm not htinking frrely with an empty mind, so to speak. A mind free fro preconceptions and has a ability to acoomodate new thoughts, new facts and analyze them upto full strength. 
 One more point hwich should be discussed, is that emotions are very key part of any persons which makes a person a human a living thing. If we go with the emotional apprach rather then a logical approach there is very less chance to get confused and it gives us more strength. Later anyway logical analysis will be done on the decision. 
 In short emotions reduces our capability to analyse all possiblle view honestly. Just taking one view supported by our emotion and analyzing it is not suffice. A decision based on partial thinking has a high probability of failing or being wrong. And justifying the decision with logic just becomes a mere formality. Hence it makes a decision maker poor.
feedback:
The essay demonstrates an understanding of the significance of emotion in decision-making processes, presenting a perspective that emphasizes the limitations of emotional influence on logical analysis. However, there are several areas where improvements could significantly enhance the essay's effectiveness and clarity.

1. **Response to the Topic**: It's crucial to accurately introduce the essay topic at the beginning to set a clear foundation for the discussion. The essay's introduction does not fully encapsulate the prompt, making it somewhat vague for the reader to grasp the full extent of the discussion to follow. A more explicit paraphrasing or depiction of the prompt would benefit the essay by giving readers a better understanding of the context and the arguments' direction.

2. **Cohesion and Coherence**: The essay somewhat lacks cohesion, particularly in how ideas are structured and presented. The second paragraph introduces an example but fails to fully elaborate on it, which disrupts the flow of information and makes the argument feel disjointed. Ensuring that each paragraph fully explores and concludes its thoughts before moving on to the next can enhance readability and comprehension. A cohesive thought process, where ideas smoothly transition from one to the next without forcing the reader to revisit previous sections for clarification, is key to maintaining the reader's engagement and understanding.

3. **Spelling and Grammar**: Attention to detail in spelling and grammar is vital for conveying professionalism and respect for the reader. The essay contains several spelling errors and grammatical issues that could distract the reader and detract from the argument's credibility. Examples include "emotionally" misspelled as "emotinally" and incorrect usage of phrases (e.g., "frrely" should be "freely"). Investing time in proofreading and editing to identify and correct such errors is essential. Reading your essay multiple times before submission can help catch these mistakes, ensuring that your essay is polished and well-presented.

4. **Clarity and Precision**: The point of view is generally clear, with a stance that emotional decision-making can undermine the objectivity and thoroughness of logical analysis. However, the arguments supporting this position could be more descriptive and precise. When providing examples or making assertions, ensuring that each point is thoroughly explained and connected back to the main argument can strengthen the essay's persuasiveness. Aim to explicitly state how each piece of evidence supports your viewpoint to make your position unmistakably clear to the reader.

5. **Balanced Arguments**: While the essay convincingly argues the limitations of emotional decision-making, considering and briefly acknowledging the potential benefits of emotions in decision-making could provide a more balanced view. This acknowledgment does not detract from your argument but rather adds depth, showing a comprehensive understanding of the topic.

In summary, focusing on accurately introducing the topic, improving cohesion and coherence, diligently checking spelling and grammar, enhancing the clarity and descriptiveness of arguments, and providing a balanced perspective will significantly improve the essay. These adjustments will not only make the essay more compelling but also demonstrate a greater level of care and respect for the reader's experience.

prompt: IELTES Task 2: Online shopping is replacing shopping in stores.
essay:
Nowadays, we can see the population of online shopping is increasing, therefore the behavior of purchasing things in physical store has declined. In this essay I will argue that the idea of online shopping is a positive development. 
 First of all, purchasing merchandises online can make our life more efficient. Take shopping for grocery for example, it is a laborious chores for me to buy grocery in person. It takes time driving between home and supermarket, also a waste of time to walk around supermarket try to find the items on my shopping list. But the situation would have been so different for searching items by type in keywords and click quantity I need then the items I bought online will be on my doorstep the next day. Shopping online saves me a lot of time and allow me to spend my time on more meaningful thing. 
 In addition, on the aspect of a supermarket owner, online shopping will reduce the rent. Renting a place for supermarket is usually much more expensive due to the location has to be on the spot that as convenient for people to go to as possible, this fact makes the rent is general higher than rent a storehouse. For the owners of online shopping stores, then can rent a storehouse and it is not necessary to located in the urban center. Thus the shop owners can costdown and reflect on the price. Then the consumer are more likely to chose them in order to have a good bargain. 
 In conclusion, shopping online not only make our lives efficiency but also more likely for consumer to have a good deal for their shopping. Thus, my opinion for this essay is that it is a positive development of online shopping replacing shopping in physical store.
feedback:
The essay addresses the topic of online shopping as a positive development, which is a response to the given prompt. However, the introduction of strong emotions or assertive statements like "I will argue" is not necessary. Essays of this nature focus more on discussing various viewpoints rather than debating, so aim for a more neutral tone. For instance, starting with a statement such as "These days, shopping has evolved from physical stores to virtual platforms. Some view this as a positive trend, while others may see it as negative. Here, the reasons for considering it a beneficial development will be explored." This approach is more in line with the expectations of the essay task.

Regarding spelling and grammar, there are no significant spelling errors, but some grammatical and stylistic improvements can be made. For example, "the population of online shopping is increasing" might be better expressed as "the popularity of online shopping is increasing." Additionally, phrases like "laborious chores" and "costdown" could be clarified as "laborious chore" and "reduce costs," respectively, to improve readability.

The point of view is clear throughout the essay; however, expressing it in a less direct manner and avoiding emotional responses can enhance the essay's professionalism. Aim for a balanced perspective even when supporting a specific viewpoint.

The arguments provided, like the efficiency of online shopping and cost savings for business owners, effectively support the viewpoint. Nevertheless, specificity in examples could be broadened beyond grocery shopping, aligning with the advice to avoid focusing on a particular sector unless the prompt specifies one. Discussing the advantages of online shopping in general terms, such as mentioning the broader benefits for various types of online stores and their customers, could make the argument more persuasive and applicable to a wider range of scenarios.

In the segment discussing the advantages for business owners, the essay could benefit from a more detailed explanation emphasizing the financial and operational efficiencies of online shopping platforms. This could include how virtual storefronts save on rental costs and allow for a potentially broader customer base without the need for a expensive, high-traffic physical location.

The conclusion summarizes the main points effectively; however, reiterating the benefits mentioned while subtly acknowledging the broader impacts of online shopping on society could provide a more comprehensive wrap-up.

Overall, the essay makes a solid argument in favor of online shopping being a positive development. By adjusting the tone, expanding the scope of examples, and refining the grammatical structure, the essay could be improved in clarity, persuasiveness, and alignment with the task's requirements.

prompt: Information in a specific sector should be shared freely or not. Discuss both these views and give your own opinion
essay:
Scientific research, business and the academic world had been changing in the past decades. There some information had been discovered which gives a huge impact to development. Some people believe that is very importantto share as much information, in other hand some people believe that there are some information which classified and do not need to share. I strongly believe that the information should not be shared freely. 
 The information in scientific research, business or the academic world is very important. These informations perhaps for some people is a dangerous or if these informations shared that will gives a wrong speculate among people. I believe these can be reduced with spesific laws which can control in distribution. The government it self, should select the information which will be shared carefully, because it will be a catastrophe if the government gives a wrong informations. For example, in my country the government announce about the new energy which can be used as a safer energy called nuclear. Whereas, these informations become a pro and contra in society. The people who does not have clear information about that become paranoid and get scared easily. 
 In other hand, I do believe gives as much information as possible also a great action to support and increase a quality of people's life. also, The easier people gets the information become a factor to measure about the quality of nation. for example, Europe as a develop country which each people can get all the information easily, the government provides the access of the information in every corner of the street, so people in rural area also can search information for develop their land. This sample do not happens in my country which a developing country, people in this situation always have a limited solution for their problem because of the access of the information. 
 In conclusion, I strongly believe that should be a law or new regulation to control shared information about scientific issue, business and the academic world. The information which shared should give not only a new information but also safe feeling for everybody.
feedback:
Your essay outlines a perspective on information sharing within scientific research, business, and academia, emphasizing the potential dangers of unrestricted dissemination and advocating for controlled distribution through specific laws. However, to enhance your essay and ensure it aligns more closely with the expectations of an English proficiency test, consider the following feedback:

1. **Response to the Topic:** The essay partially addresses the prompt by discussing the importance of regulated information sharing. However, it leans heavily towards a singular viewpoint, emphasizing the risks associated with free dissemination. The task requires a balanced exploration of both perspectives—those in favor of open information sharing and those against it, culminating in your reasoned opinion. Ensuring that you understand and address all parts of the prompt will significantly improve your essay's relevance and effectiveness.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, or Stylistic Issues:** There are several areas in need of improvement:
    - **Spelling and Grammar**: Pay attention to plural forms (e.g., "informations" should be "information" as it is uncountable in English) and subject-verb agreement (e.g., "gives" should be "give"). Additionally, be mindful of word choice and sentence construction to improve clarity.
    - **Stylistic Issues**: Your essay would benefit from a more formal tone and consistent use of third-person narration, avoiding colloquial expressions and maintaining an academic style throughout.

3. **Clarity of Author's Perspective:** Your stance is clear; however, it's presented without adequately considering the alternative perspective. Offering a balanced discussion before concluding with your personal viewpoint would not only strengthen your argument but also align with the expectations of a well-rounded essay.

4. **Arguments and Support:** While your essay states your viewpoint, it lacks detailed arguments and examples to substantiate your position comprehensively. Consider including:
    - **For Both Sides**: Specific examples or scenarios where sharing/not sharing information had significant outcomes.
    - **For Your Opinion**: Clear, detailed reasons supporting your belief, bolstered by examples or data, will make your argument more persuasive.
    
To construct your essay effectively, structure it as follows: 
   - Begin with a paraphrase of the prompt.
   - Discuss the first viewpoint and support it with statements and examples, followed by a transition sentence.
   - Explore the opposing view similarly, providing supporting evidence.
   - Present your personal opinion, underpinned by reasons and examples.
   - Conclude by summarizing the discussed points and restating your opinion.

This structured approach ensures that your essay fully addresses the prompt, presenting a comprehensive and balanced analysis before articulating your informed stance. Continuous practice, alongside careful consideration of these aspects, will undoubtedly contribute to improved performance on your English proficiency test.

prompt: IELTS writing task 2: Teenage and adult life as the happiest time of lives
essay:
Some think that youth is the best time of their lives, while others argue old age was the happiest. While it is more acceptable due to exploring themselves by trying new experiences at school and career, I believe in adults enjoying life as a result of their hard work. 
 The happiness of youth striving for education and career is something memorable towards a successful future. Youth struggle especially with colleagues is certainly an unforgettable beautiful moment, however it will be remembered as pleasant moments for everyone. An Indonesian successful lawyer always remembers his youth as the happiest moment. During his master's and doctoral education in Australia was unforgettable moments, especially when he had challenging case projects with his colleagues such as the joyful moments when they had successfully completed it as his first international experience. I believe behind someone's success there must be the happiest moment that can't be seen by others, the struggle of youth is one of them. 
 The satisfaction and financial adequacy were valued strong reasons to enjoy life in old age, especially to face greater responsibilities as parents. A successful future was actually the happiest goal after the hard effort they had, failure in youth is a reason for success in the future. For instance, an Indonesian businessman namely Rico Huang got his success after several failures. Rico started his business by selling various foods, but due to limited knowledge in the culinary, finally he lost even up to 15 million in just 4 days. But then he switched to mobile phone businesses and selling them through various social media. Finally, this marketing strategy gets a positive response from market demand and ensures his old age to be a happier life. 
 In conclusion, many youngster enjoy their time to encourage themselves to be great people, while receiving income and enjoying old age quietly will bring more happiness. However, joyful moments depend on what makes us comfortable and happy, sometimes it doesn't matter what age it is.
feedback:
The essay has done a commendable job in addressing the topic by presenting views on the happiness experienced during youth and old age. However, to enhance the essay, focusing on structure, clarity, and depth of argumentation will be beneficial.

Firstly, the essay’s response to the topic is generally clear, but it needs to be more focused. The introduction briefly mentions the contrasting views on which period of life is happier but could more explicitly state the essay’s stance. The conclusion also attempts to summarize the points made but introduces new ideas that weren't fully explored. It's essential to ensure that all opinions in the essay are fully supported by subsequent paragraphs.

In terms of spelling and grammar, the essay is mostly accurate. Nonetheless, there are instances where sentence structure could be improved for better readability. For example, complex sentences sometimes make understanding the point challenging. Splitting these into simpler sentences may help maintain clarity. Additionally, paying attention to the correct use of past and present tense will enhance the essay's overall professionalism.

The essay author's point of view is present but could be more strongly articulated. Aligning with the feedback on structure, it is advisable to dedicate a separate paragraph to clearly articulate and support the personal opinion. This approach will help in presenting a well-rounded argument and avoid reducing the effectiveness of your stance. Remember, each paragraph should ideally open with a sentence that introduces the key argument or idea, followed by explanations or examples.

Regarding the arguments supporting the author's position, the essay provides interesting anecdotes to illustrate points. However, the arguments could be more descriptive and detailed. For instance, when discussing the happiness derived from youth struggles, incorporating a broader range of examples or exploring psychological or societal reasons could enrich the argument. Similarly, the discussion on old age happiness benefits from more in-depth analysis beyond financial success stories. Exploring aspects such as emotional maturity, fulfillment from life's achievements, or the joy of seeing younger generations flourish could add depth to your argument.

Lastly, it's essential to format your essay properly by ensuring each paragraph discusses a single main idea and transitions smoothly to the next. This includes avoiding attaching a single-line personal opinion at the end of the essay without proper development. Any personal views should be thoroughly explored within the body of the essay to effectively support your stance.

By addressing these areas, your essay will not only provide a more engaging read but also present a compelling argument that accurately reflects your views. Remember, the key to a successful essay lies in clarity, coherence, and a well-supported argument.

prompt: Different views about what kinds of obligation a company should have
essay:
People have different views about what kinds of obligation a company should have. While I agree that the top priority of companies is to generate profits, I believe that they should also take responsibilities for society. 
 On the one hand, for most the company, making money is the precursor in an attempt to survive in a competitive world. Firstly, any companies have to cover its running cost such as employee's salary packages and payments for buildings and utilities. If a company is unable to pay its bills or meet the changing needs of customers, it would face the possibility of the verge of collapse. Secondly, when enterprises earn much money they can broaden their businesses, which opens avenues to seek more employment opportunities for people securing a job. To exemplify, some international corporations and firms such as Apple or Microsoft has been employing hundreds of thousands individuals around the world, which catalyzes to reduce unemployment rates and bolster economy growth in many countries. 
 On the other hand, there are several reasons why businesses should also accept that they have social responsibilities. The first one is that serious environmental damage is mainly attributed to the rising number of facilities. Therefore, they need to take immediately actions to protect the natural environment. Indeed, they could try new technologies to recycle their wastes instead of disposing them immediately. Another reason is that businesses could use a proportion of their profits to support local charities, especially needy people and those who are less fortunate such as homeless and disabled citizens. It promotes a sense of humanity and leads to the fact that public image of enterprises would be universally popularized. 
 In conclusion, although I believe that companies prioritize to make profits, I favour that they should have social responsibilities.
feedback:
Your essay shows an understanding of the broad topic, engaging with the idea that companies have responsibilities both to generate profits and to society. However, the task required you to choose one stance and support it with evidence, rather than discussing both perspectives. This approach may lead to misunderstanding the given instructions, and it's important for future essays to closely adhere to the specific requirements of the assignment to ensure accuracy and relevance.

Regarding spelling, grammar, and stylistic issues:
- Pay attention to subject-verb agreement ("companies have" instead of "any companies have," "has been employing" should be "have been employing").
- Be mindful of article usage ("the company" rather than "most the company", and consider "running costs" instead of "running cost").
- Look out for singular and plural consistency ("take immediately actions" should be "take immediate action").

Your point of view is present, indicating that companies should not only focus on profit but also engage in social responsibilities. However, clarifying and emphasizing your stance at the beginning can strengthen the reader's understanding of your essay’s direction.

The arguments you present are generally clear and provide a basis for your viewpoint. To improve, consider the following:
- Be specific in examples and make sure they directly support your stance. For example, when mentioning technologies for recycling, detailing how this directly impacts society or the economy can strengthen your argument.
- Ensure your examples are accurately supporting the argument you are making. For the argument about employment opportunities through companies like Apple or Microsoft, ensure it is clear how this supports your view on societal responsibilities.

Lastly, in your conclusion, reaffirming your stance with a brief summary of your key points will make your argument more compelling. Remember, adhering to the essay instructions closely and choosing a clear stance from the start can significantly strengthen your essay. Keep focusing on clear, precise language and structuring your essay to directly address the prompt.

prompt: Writing task 2: Argument essay (Food and Diet)
essay:
In this day and age, it is argued that everyone had better be in a vegetarian diet which is without meat to keep their health in good condition. While I agree that people should become vegetarians , I believe that meat is necessary for our meals. 
 On the one hand, there are a number of reasons why people ought to become vegetarians. First, a vegetarian diet might be high in healthy nutrients, such as vitamins, fibers and minerals, which could be beneficial for the immune system. For instance, being in a vegetarian diet encourages people to eat more vegetables which are helpful to build up their immunity and avoid diseases. Second, because of not eating meat, many people could easily lose their weight by following a low calories diet. For example, people who are overweight would quickly get slimmer body and flatter their physical appearances by these diets. 
 On the other hand, I believe that the diet which there is no meat could bring lots of harmfulness to our health . First, since there might be not enough nutrients for the metabolism of the body, that lack could cause a lot of digestive troubles. In fact, vegetarians who do not eat meat would be easily suffering from the needy of proteins, which harm to the muscle system. Second, due to not eating meat, many people are not likely to provide adequate energy for their daily routines. This means that if people have a diet without meat, they would not study or work effectively. 
 In conclusion, while having a vegetarian diet could bring a lot of benefits for our health, I believe that meat would be extremely essential for the diet.
feedback:
The essay discusses the debate surrounding a vegetarian diet versus a diet that includes meat, presenting arguments for both sides before concluding that meat is necessary. Here's how the essay can be improved:

1. **Relevance to the Topic**: The introduction starts with a phrase "In this day and age" which could be seen as a memorized phrase often used by English language learners. Instead, start with a clear statement about the topic. For example: "The health benefits of vegetarian diets as compared to diets including meat are widely debated." This directly addresses the topic without implying a broad argument.

2. **Clarity of Opinion**: The essay attempts to present a balanced view by discussing the benefits of both vegetarian and meat-inclusive diets, but an essay should express a clear point of view. When the essay claims agreement with both sides, it dilutes the argument. Choose a definitive stance and construct the essay around it. It's essential to clearly support one perspective over the other to meet the essay's requirements.

3. **Grammar and Style**: There are minor grammatical errors and some sentences could be more concise for clarity. For instance, the sentence "For instance, being in a vegetarian diet encourages people to eat more vegetables which are helpful to build up their immunity and avoid diseases." could be simplified to "For instance, a vegetarian diet, rich in vegetables, can enhance immunity and prevent diseases."

4. **Argument Development**: While the essay provides reasons and examples for both diets, the arguments could be more descriptive and backed by concrete examples or data. For example, stating specific studies or statistics about the health benefits or drawbacks of vegetarian and meat-based diets would strengthen the arguments.

5. **Conclusion Improvement**: The conclusion should not only restate the personal stance but also succinctly summarize the arguments made throughout the essay. Additionally, relate the conclusion back to the original topic more directly to reinforce the relevance of the arguments presented. A more forceful conclusion would reiterate the chosen stance and summarize why it is preferred over the alternative, based on the arguments provided.

6. **Avoid Comparative Discussion**: The essay tries to weigh both diets equally. However, for this type of essay, focusing on advocating for one side based on the requirements would be more appropriate. Highlight why one diet is preferable, supporting this view with logical reasoning and evidence.

7. **Spelling and Punctuation**: Always proofread to catch any spelling mistakes or punctuation errors. These small errors can distract from the argument and make the essay appear less polished.

By focusing on these areas of improvement, the essay can become more cohesive, persuasive, and aligned with the prompt.

prompt: Some people think that environmental problems should be solved on a global scale while others believe it is better to deal with them nationally. Discuss both sides and give your opinion.
essay:
Nowadays, issues related to environment have grabbed many people's attentions. While some people believe there will be more efficient if countries integrate into solving this problem, others think that each country is more likely to find its own causes and apply appropriate measures. To my way of thinking, I realize that it will be better to solve environmental problems on both global and national scale. 
 On the one hand, it is undeniable that it will be necessary for all of countries to explore their own methods reducing negative effects on our surroundings for various reasons. Firstly, each country currently has been facing its own environmental issues which may be different from others. Therefore, it is probably more convenient if countries, in a way, search for their own causes thus implementing some policies to alleviate certain problems. To some extent, dealing with detrimental impacts on environment independently, more or less, helps governmental offices manage and control more easily. Hence, the government can also contribute to spread a plenty of campaigns about environmental protection. 
 On the other hand, there is mounting evidence that solving problems about environment on global scale possibly brings more merits for countries. In our fast-changing world, there are many developed nations launching programs to stimulate interest in keeping the environment from harm as leaders. As a consequence of those campaigns, others probably follow and learn more about numerous methods to apply for their own countries. Furthermore, co-operation among many countries around the world may trigger to internationally environmental workshops. Those seminars focusing on young generations have been giving them more chance to enhance their abilities and be aware of global concerns thus gradually creating a strong network of handling environmental problems. 
 In conclusion, based on what I have analyzed, I believe that countries all over the world should have a hand in preventing as well as protecting the environment.
feedback:
The essay effectively addresses the topic by discussing the importance of both national and global approaches to solving environmental issues. However, there are areas for improvement to better meet the essay prompt's requirements and enhance overall clarity and coherence.

Firstly, the structure of the essay could be more aligned with the expectation of a 5-paragraph format, focusing on two different viewpoints and a personal opinion. It's important to clearly distinguish between the two public opinions and your own perspective. Each opinion should be presented in separate paragraphs, beginning with the viewpoint you are less in favor of. Use specific references to “some people think…” or “others believe…” to clearly identify these as public opinions, not your own, before introducing your personal stance.

Secondly, your essay would benefit from a clearer separation between the summary of public opinions and your own thoughts. Your personal opinion deserves its own paragraph, where you can tie it back to the arguments presented earlier, supporting it with strong reasons. Following this, the conclusion should succinctly summarize the discussion without introducing new information. A well-structured conclusion would restate the main topic, briefly recap the presented opinions, reiterate your perspective, and end with a closing sentence. Remember, the purpose of the conclusion is to wrap up the discussion, not to introduce new points.

Regarding grammar and style, your essay is mostly clear, but there are minor issues that could be polished for better readability. Pay attention to repetitive sentence structures and try to vary your language to make your arguments more compelling. Additionally, proofread for minor spelling and grammatical errors that, while not greatly hindering understanding, can distract from the strength of your argument.

Despite these points of feedback, it's evident that you have a good understanding of the topic and can successfully communicate your ideas. Your discussion shows awareness of the complexities surrounding environmental issues and highlights the potential benefits of both localized and global efforts. By focusing on refining the structure of your essay and sharpening your arguments, you have the potential to create a more powerful and persuasive piece.

Keep refining your work, as practice will greatly enhance your clarity and effectiveness in writing. Your ability to discuss complex issues is a strength, and with some adjustments to the structure and presentation of your essay, you can achieve an even higher level of proficiency.

prompt: How to save our society? Honour, knowledge, honesty, and humanity.
essay:
In recent years, the societies have changed in many cultural and economical aspects. Some people grab the idea that individuals should be categorised and valued by their social status, their jobs and their wealth. They say that the materialistic view could be a good way to identifying people's level and honour, kindness and other ethical characters are out of date. I am totally against this idea and view and in this essay I will explain my reasons. 
 First, I believe the characters such and being gentle, honesty, living by honour and following the conscience exist from the begging the life on the earth and never lost their value. Good manners and kindness were the basic requirements for any leaders and communities where followed ethical codes remained in the history. Even in the all holy books, there are stories of those who followed the humanity and their good name remained even after they vanished from the earth. 
 Secondly, in our current society, everything changes rapidly and those things which were worthy and loved by many, have been forgotten. I think first character of a good measurement should be its lasting and if something yesterday existed and today has gone, could not be considered as a good index. Wealth, social status and other material possessions could be lost as time goes on. A rich person might lose the job or went bankrupt and become no one in the society with materialistic measures. And in contrary, A thief or a liar could change to be a very well-known person because of his of her material-possessions and this could change all the basics of the ethics in the community. Of course I do not say that all rich or famous people are inhuman or unkind, just making a short discussion about the value of humanity. 
 In the other side, there are who see the high positions and expensive cars or houses as an index to judge about people. They say money would bring power and change many things. With money and power, one can run the country and lead it to the better way. 
 In sum, I believe, honour, knowledge, honesty and living by humanity would save our society and last forever.
feedback:
Your essay provides a clear stance against the valuation of individuals based on materialistic aspects, favoring moral qualities such as honor and honesty. However, there are several areas where improvements are crucial for aligning your essay more closely with English proficiency test standards.

1. **Understanding and Responding to the Essay Prompt:**
It's important to precisely respond to the essay's prompt. Your expression of being "totally against" the idea slightly diverts from the expected response to either agree or disagree with the given statement. Focusing on directly stating whether you agree or disagree can improve clarity and task accuracy, which is a critical aspect of the evaluation.

2. **Grammar and Sentence Structure:**
There are significant issues with grammar and sentence construction that hinder comprehension. Sentences like "First, I believe the characters such and being gentle, honesty, living by honour and from the begging the life on the earth..." could be rephrased for clarity and grammatical accuracy. For instance, "First, I believe that qualities such as gentleness, honesty, and living honorably have been valued since the beginning of time..." Practicing simple and complex sentence structures while adhering to grammatical rules will enhance the readability of your essay.

3. **Vocabulary and Lexical Resource:**
The essay sometimes uses terms or phrases ambiguously, which could confuse readers. Focusing on precise word choice and understanding the nuances of vocabulary you employ will contribute to a clearer and more effective communication of your ideas. Expanding your lexical resource through reading and practice will enable you to express complex ideas more succinctly.

4. **Cohesion and Coherence:**
While your essay presents several arguments against the valuation of individuals by materialistic measures, enhancing the organization and flow of your ideas would improve its coherence. Making use of transition words and phrases to connect your thoughts more effectively can guide the reader through your argumentation more smoothly.

5. **Developing Arguments:**
Your essay would benefit from clearer and more detailed arguments supporting your viewpoint. When presenting an idea, try to follow it with specific examples or evidence that reinforces your stance. This not only bolsters your argument but also demonstrates your ability to analyze and synthesize information.

Going forward, take time to plan your essay by outlining your main arguments before you start writing. This can help ensure that your essay remains on topic and that each paragraph contributes effectively to your overall thesis. Additionally, reviewing grammar rules and expanding your vocabulary through reading can significantly improve your writing skills. Remember, improvement in essay writing is a gradual process, and persistence is key.

prompt: WRITING TASK 2: Should government funding be used for new house instead of restoring old building ?
essay:
Arguments regarding cities' infrastructure developments have gained much contemporary attention in recent years. A large amount of public budget being allocated for old buildings' refurbishments has raised much opposition since it is believed to be more worthy to spend such funding on building more houses and roads. From my perspective, I believe that the government funding should not be utterly focused on any development, but should be evenly divided for both such purposes. 
 To commence with, constructing modern buildings and roads is claimed to be a superior mission for a range of factors. The first underlying reason behind this proposal is the proliferating demands of accommodations in metropolises nowadays. Owing to the urbanization occurring significantly in cities, dramatic escalations have been recorded in the numbers of urban populations and as a result, more houses are needed in order to accommodate citizens. Besides, establishing new streets may deliver social advantages, as well as the improvement in road safety. As new roads are introduced in municipalities, city-dwellers are presented with a plethora of options to travel during rush hours, which results in a significant reduction in the rate of traffic jams. Moreover, the figures of road accidents can be mitigated once overcrowding in particular roads is eradicated. 
 On the other hand, renovating antique buildings in cities should also be placed emphasis on for several reasons. Firstly, such old institutions can become promising sources of income for cities. According to a survey, foreign travelers are inclined to visit and take pictures of local ancient and historical places; therefore, old buildings can be seen as cities' potential tourist sites. As a result, huge revenues can be made regularly by commercializing such constructions. Secondly, historical institutions can heavily stimulate artists' inner creativity with their antique beauty and underlying stories. For instance, a rich variety of pieces of fine arts were inspired by the image and structure, as well as the history of the famous One Pillar Pagoda in Hanoi and undoubtedly, they are highly appreciated and acclaimed by both the public and the critics. 
 In conclusion, weighing both sides of the argument, I wholeheartedly believe that constructing new houses and roads as well as restoring old institutions are equally crucial in the development of cities and therefore, even amounts of money should be spent on both purposes.
feedback:
Your essay effectively addresses the topic, showcasing a clear understanding of the issues surrounding city infrastructure development. Your ability to present balanced views on allocating funds for new construction and the refurbishment of old buildings demonstrates a thoughtful analysis of the subject. The arguments provided are compelling and well-supported, highlighting both the need for accommodating growing urban populations and preserving historical buildings for cultural and economic benefits.

However, there are areas for improvement that would make your essay even stronger. While your command of the English language is commendable, aiming for conciseness and clarity would enhance your writing. Essays for proficiency tests often have word count and time limits, so it's crucial to express your ideas as clearly and directly as possible. Try to refine your writing by sticking to a limit of 3-5 sentences per paragraph, directly addressing the point. This practice will help ensure that your essay is focused and to the point, eliminating unnecessary details.

Another aspect to consider is the structure and presentation of your essay. Ensure that your essay is well-organized, with a clear introduction, body paragraphs that each focus on a single point, and a concise conclusion that summarizes your position. This structure will help you meet the "C + C requirements" (clarity and conciseness) and make your essay more effective.

Lastly, while your essay has minimal grammatical errors, it's always beneficial to allocate time for proofreading. Spending the last few minutes reviewing your work can help you catch and correct any mistakes, ensuring your essay is polished and error-free.

In summary, focusing on clarity, conciseness, and careful organization will strengthen your essay. Your ability to analyze and discuss the topic is evident; refining your presentation will make your arguments even more powerful.

prompt: In the future all cars, buses and trucks will be driverless. Advantages outweigh the disadvantages?
essay:
The debate relating to transportation has been in the spotlight for an extended period of time. Recently, there is a belief that automated vehicles like cars, buses and trucks will be dominated in the future. From a personal perspective, such trend, though detrimental to a certain extent, could be considered as a major step forward. 
 On the one hand, driverless system of transportation has proven itself to be adverse. To begin with, personal driver's security may be deteriorated. In fact, many criminals now are gradually having more sophisticated and cunning plans in stealing the important information of other people. Thus, just by successfully accessing the storage systems, hacker can take the necessary data and control over a whole engine, easily triggering accidents on the road. Moreover, this trend may cause the unemployment of people. To illustrate, thousands of individuals are likely to lose their job as a taxi driver or bus controller since all the vehicles are substituted by automated ones, which may affect negatively their quality of life. 
 On the other hand, this trend seems to be advantageous from two aspects, as follows. First, human's safety will be guaranteed. There is no denying that, the majority number of accidents nowadays are attributed to drivers' inferior awareness like using stimulants or crossing the traffic light. Therefore, driverless vehicles are promised to decrease the unwanted cases as they can automatically control the speed themselves and utilize computer based system to avoid collisions. Second, this trend may bring about a civilized traffic system. The fact is that, there will no longer have traffic congestion and need police on the road since no more human could break the rules and the distance between two cars might be rigorously calculated in order to minimize collisions. 
 In conclusion, in spite of several demerits mentioned above, this essay still believe that putting more driverless vehicles on the road is revolutionary to the contemporary society.
feedback:
This essay provides an engaging look at the debate over automated vehicles, exploring both the potential benefits and drawbacks. However, there are several areas where improvements could make your arguments clearer and more persuasive.

1. **Response to the Topic**: The essay begins by broadly addressing the debate on transportation, which isn't directly focused on the essay question. It's crucial to directly respond to the question asked. For instance, if the question is about whether the advantages of driverless vehicles outweigh the disadvantages, your thesis statement should clearly reflect your stance on this specific issue. Instead of starting with a general discussion, immediately state your opinion on the pros and cons of automated vehicles to make your position clear from the beginning.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, and Style**: There are minor spelling and grammar issues throughout your essay that can detract from its overall clarity and professionalism. For example, phrases like "majority number" should be "majority of accidents," and "thus, just by successfully accessing" could be simplified to "thus, by accessing." Remember to review your verb tenses for consistency and ensure that your sentences are complete. Breaking complex ideas into shorter sentences can also enhance readability and comprehension.

3. **Clarity of Point of View**: Your essay presents a balanced view but leans towards supporting driverless vehicles. To strengthen your argument, it's important to more clearly state your overall point of view early in the essay. This will help guide your readers through your arguments and make your conclusion more persuasive.

4. **Arguments and Support**: You've provided arguments for both sides of the debate but combining them into one paragraph for positives and one for negatives causes some loss of focus. Consider separating your key points into distinct paragraphs with clear topic sentences that announce the specific argument being made. This structure will make your essay easier to follow and your arguments more impactful.

Additionally, while you've discussed important issues like safety and unemployment, incorporating evidence, like studies or statistics, could further strengthen your arguments. For example, mentioning research on the safety of driverless cars or data on projected job losses could make your points more compelling.

To improve your essay:
- Directly answer the essay question in your introduction, clearly stating whether you believe the advantages of driverless vehicles outweigh the disadvantages.
- Review and correct minor spelling and grammar issues.
- Use distinct paragraphs for each argument, ensuring each has a clear topic sentence.
- Enhance your arguments with specific evidence where possible.

Remember that practice and revision are key components of writing improvement. Keep engaging with the topic, refining your arguments, and your writing will continue to grow stronger.

prompt: Tax private car owners to improve public transportation - ADVANTAGE AND DISADVANTAGE?
essay:
In order to deal with the current dense volume of traffic, the Vietnamese government suggests a policy in which private car owners should be charged a high fee to improve public transportation. This policy would lead to certain benefits such as reducing traffic density and improving air quality but there are also drawbacks such as causing inconveniences due to the unavailability of public transportation. 
 On the one hand, the policy would help reduce traffic density and improve air quality. This is because when people are charged a high fee every time they use their cars, they only use them when it is necessary or when they are in emergencies, which would cut down on the number of cars at one time, particularly during rush hours, and ease the traffic density. Additionally, some would choose public transportation for remote purposes instead to avoid paying the tax. This, thus, ends up lower the amount of CO2 emitted into the environment and boosts the overall air quality of the country. 
 On the other hand, the policy may cause several certain inconveniences due to the unavailability of public transportation. Many areas in Vietnam do not have access to public transportation. Therefore, some can be placed in a situation where they do not have any choice but to use their private cars. For example, one lives in a small town that doesn't have any bus stop or train stations. He/she has to use his/her private car to go to work and pay a heavy tax for using car. However, he/she doesn't have a chance to use public transportation and save money spent on the tax like others, which is quite unfair for him/her. 
 In conclusion, the policy of taxing private car owners to improve public transportation can help alleviate the volume of traffic and air pollution in Vietnam. Nonetheless, this also leads to some inconveniences because of the unavailability of public transportation. To enhance the efficiency of the policy, the government should widen the public transportation system first to make sure everyone can access them.
feedback:
Your essay provides a thoughtful discussion on the impacts of charging private car owners a high fee to improve public transportation. The points you raise about reducing traffic density and improving air quality, along with addressing potential inconveniences, are well-considered. However, there are several areas where improvements could be made to enhance the clarity, accuracy, and persuasiveness of your essay.

1. **Topic Response Accuracy**: The essay represents the idea as being specific to the Vietnamese government, which deviates from the original topic's general perspective. When discussing policies or proposals, it's important to accurately reflect the premise provided in the prompt. To improve, ensure your essay aligns closely with the topic as presented.

2. **Gender Reference in English Writing**: In English, it's common to use a singular gender pronoun ("he") when the gender is unknown or not specified. Your attempt to include both genders ("he/she") in your essay, while inclusive, might not be necessary and can make your writing appear more complex than required. Simplifying this aspect could make your essay clearer and easier to follow.

3. **Conviction and Examples**: Your arguments, while logical, would benefit greatly from stronger examples and more detailed explanations. Including specific, real-world examples can enhance the persuasiveness of your argument by showing the practical outcomes of the policy you're discussing. Try to incorporate at least one detailed example to reinforce your main points.

4. **Focus on the Prompt's Scope**: It's essential that your conclusion and the entire discussion reflect the general scope of the original prompt, rather than focusing on a specific government, unless the prompt specifies it. This ensures your essay maintains a broad perspective that's in line with the task's requirements.

5. **Language and Style Improvements**: Watch out for minor spelling, grammar, and stylistic issues. For instance, paying attention to article use and making sure your sentences flow well can improve the readability of your essay.

6. **Clarity of Point of View**: Your essay does a good job of presenting a clear point of view. Continuing to clearly state your opinion and back it up with logical reasoning and evidence will strengthen your argumentation.

In summary, you've laid a good foundation with your essay by presenting a clear opinion and supporting it with logical arguments. To enhance your essay, focus on accurately addressing the prompt, simplifying your language, offering stronger and more specific examples, and ensuring your discussion remains within the scope of the general topic. These improvements will make your essay more compelling and coherent.

prompt: Task 2 ielts - sports and exercise classes and academic subjects. Discuss the effect of this trend
essay:
The past few years have witnessed a dramatic increase in proportion of subjects. In many countries ,Some people think that students should extend their understanding beyond the boundaries of sports and exercise classes which are replaced with academic subjects .Based on my experiences and observation, as far as I concerned that this thought is not completely justifiable. 
 Firstly, sports and exercise classes are the good method to train character and life skills. Needless to say, sports have taught us how important cooperation is. For example, when ones play football, not only are ones talent, but also ones need how to combine with anyone else, because the more competitive ones are, the more lower score ones get. Ones need cooperation to build plans and formation. If someone on the team does not associate with each other, that team will lose. Not only do sports need it, but also one's life, one'swork and community even need it to exist. Moreover, sports and exercise classes built good health and flexibility. Nowadays, when advanced technology which are encouraging a lot of benefits have been invented , so ones are too lazy to do exercise .Consequently , Ones have to suffer from so many diseases such as diaries , cancers , obesity , ... we need to do exercise to prevent those things from our health. 
 Although, sports and exercise classes are really beneficial and healthy, we should not spend all our time devoting for them. Ones have to learn academics subjects such as science to solve unresolved problems. For example, if ones want to know where diseases come from or how to find the solutions to whatever drawbacks, ones need to have knowledge from academic subjects ones have learnt. By using those knowledge, ones find out what we ones have to do. 
 To conclude, we have to separate our time for muscular class and academic subjects logically. Instead of spending all time studying, and one should spend a little time to exercise and the opposite
feedback:
The essay addresses the topic by discussing the balance between physical and academic education. However, there are several areas that could be improved to make the essay more effective.

1. **Response to the Topic**: The essay correctly addresses the debate over the importance of both academic subjects and physical education but lacks a clear structure to effectively separate and develop each argument. Consider dividing the essay into distinct paragraphs, each focusing on a different aspect of the discussion. For example, one paragraph could focus on the benefits of sports and exercise for character and life skills, and another could explore the importance of academic subjects in solving problems.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, and Style**: The essay contains several spelling and grammar errors, and the use of style could be improved. Avoid using non-standard forms like "ones" as a subject and be cautious with the use of plural and singular forms. Also, avoid using special characters such as ellipses (...) in formal writing, as they can make the essay appear cluttered.

3. **Clarity of Author's Point of View**: The point of view is somewhat clear but could be made more explicit by stating your position directly and using more precise language. Make sure each sentence contributes to your argument and avoid ambiguous statements. 

4. **Arguments and Descriptions**: The arguments presented need to be clearer and more descriptive. For example, when discussing the value of sports and exercise, provide specific examples and explain how these activities teach important life skills. Similarly, when arguing for the importance of academic subjects, detail how these subjects contribute to solving societal problems.

Additional Recommendations:
- Pay attention to punctuation and capitalization. For instance, avoid capitalizing words unnecessarily (e.g., "Consequently, ones have to suffer..."). Each sentence should start with a capital letter, and proper nouns should be capitalized.
- Aim for a more formal tone suitable for an academic essay. Simplify the language but ensure it remains formal and precise.
- Consider concluding the essay with a stronger statement that summarizes the main points and reaffirms your position on the balance between physical and academic education.

Improving these areas will make the essay more coherent, persuasive, and in line with the expectations for formal academic writing.

prompt: "Cause and effect of studying abroad" This is my IELTS task 2 (recommendations and band score)
essay:
Nowadays, studying in developed countries is becoming increasingly prevalent and being considered as a matter of great concern to a large number of students in the recent years. Therefore, valuating precisely the two aspects of this tendency is an essential part of making decision that whether or not individuals should study abroad. 
 Experience and qualifications can be anticipated as major reasons because people tend to chase good things. The first reason is that studying in totally new environment allows individuals to have access to modern educational background. Therefore, learners can acquire intensive knowledge. For example, Germany can be deemed as country having the greatest education all over the world. Bringing this advantage to learners turns Germany into one of the greatest destinations for studying. Another reason is that the importance of qualification in the world full of competition. As the matter of fact, getting a valuable qualification, especially in developed countries, will give rise to improve career prospects and the opportunities for a higher salary. By ways of illustration, getting a degree in Germany will give individuals more chances than domestic degree, in Vietnam. 
 There are several consequences which can be attributed to this trend. The most transparent effect is that reduce in rate of learned workforce. It is obvious that almost students studying abroad is very intelligent people. As the case in point, champions in Olympia contest certainly win scholarship for studying in Australia and none of them has intention to work in Vietnam after their studies. Brain drain is conducive to reducing in high quality workforce in Vietnam. Nevertheless, it is undeniable that this issue will encourage domestic education to enhance to be more competitive. 
 In conclusion, authorities should analyze carefully what causes are and how it effects in domestic society in long-term to put an end to brain drain so as to attract talents.
feedback:
The essay demonstrates a thoughtful understanding of the subject regarding studying in developed countries, addressing both benefits and drawbacks. However, there are several areas where improvement is necessary for clearer expression and stronger argumentation.

1. **Response to Topic**: Without seeing the original prompt, it's challenging to assess how well the essay meets specific requirements. Make sure the essay directly responds to all aspects of the prompt, providing a comprehensive understanding of the topic.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, and Style**: The essay contains minor spelling and grammatical errors. Pay close attention to sentence structure to avoid run-on sentences and ensure clarity. Using simpler language can help make the arguments more accessible. For instance, "valuating precisely" could be simplified to "accurately evaluating." Furthermore, the essay would benefit from more varied sentence structures to improve readability and engagement.

3. **Clarity of Author's Point of View**: The essay presents a view that recognizes both the advantages and disadvantages of studying abroad. However, strengthening the personal stance by stating it explicitly in the introduction and conclusion would enhance clarity. For example, directly stating if the author believes the benefits outweigh the negative implications could solidify the argument.

4. **Arguments and Support**: The essay introduces valid points, such as the acquisition of intensive knowledge and better career prospects due to qualifications from developed countries. However, these arguments need more detailed examples and evidence for stronger support. Furthermore, each point should be clearly stated and elaborated upon in its paragraph, adhering to a structured format—ideally, a five-paragraph essay format.

**Additional Observations**:
- **Structure**: In keeping with typical essay formats, especially for assessments like IELTS Task 2, aim for a five-paragraph structure: an introduction, three body paragraphs, and a conclusion. This structure allows for a clearer presentation of ideas.
  
- **Timing and Revision**: Practicing under timed conditions can help manage the writing process better, ensuring there's enough time to review and refine your essay. Aim to leave at least 5 minutes for review at the end.

In conclusion, refining the structure, clarification of the argument, and proofreading for language accuracy will significantly improve the quality of the essay. Keep practicing with these focuses in mind to enhance your essay writing skills.

prompt: A big chunk of people's earnings goes to purchasing material things
essay:
Studies have shown that as profit of citizens from different country increases, a big chunk of their earnings goes to purchasing material things. Personally, I believe that this is a forward to a negative growth of humans today. This article will tackle the different drawbacks of overspending money for material items. 
 One negative effect of this situation is the transformation of people to becoming materialistic. In other words, their character changes towards prioritizing happiness through material things. Furthermore, these people put too much value on money because it gives them the immediate happiness they are longing for. As a result, there is an unending desire of having more than what is already owned. For instance, mobile companies have been upgrading phones every year and a lot of people feel the need of being in the trend of the latest gadgets. 
 Another drawback is that humans set aside saving money for their future needs. People put too much of their money on other things and does not prioritize their future by saving money. This could effect when they would need emergency funds if future incidents happens. For example, when someone in their family gets sick they cannot help them because they have no savings on their account. This could mean another problem adding to the current endeavor that they are facing. 
 To conclude, the usage of money on products gives more negative effect than the benefit that one could get. They could have problems with needing money when emergency rises and they could have a materialistic character which is not helpful. These reasons outweigh the benefits of using profit for buying products.
feedback:
Your essay successfully addresses the topic by discussing the negative impacts of spending too much money on material things. However, there are ways to improve your essay for better clarity and effectiveness.

1. Clarity and Response to the Topic:
- Your opening statement is a bit confusing. Instead of saying "a forward to a negative growth of humans today," a clearer way to express your idea could be "a negative development in our modern society." This makes your stance clearer to the reader right from the beginning.
- Ensure your conclusion directly restates your position without introducing new ideas. The mention of "outweigh the benefits" seems to introduce a comparison that wasn't fully explored in your essay, making the conclusion feel less decisive. Remember, the concluding paragraph is for summarizing your points, not introducing new comparisons.

2. Spelling, Grammar, and Stylistic Issues:
- There are minor grammatical errors throughout your essay. For example, "effect" should be "affect" in the sentence discussing future emergencies. Paying attention to such details can significantly improve the readability of your essay.
- Avoid overly complex or intellectual sounding phrases if they make your sentences hard to understand. Simple, clear language often communicates your ideas more effectively.

3. Clear Point of View:
- Your point of view is clear, as you argue that spending too much on material things has negative effects. This is consistently maintained throughout your essay.

4. Descriptive Argument Support:
- You provide clear examples to support your points, such as the tendency to upgrade phones driven by materialistic desires, and the neglect of saving for the future. These examples effectively highlight the issues you're discussing.
- To enhance your argument, consider discussing not just the personal consequences but also broader social or emotional impacts. This adds depth to your essay.

Overall, your essay presents a coherent argument on the drawbacks of materialism. By clarifying your thesis statement, simplifying your language, correcting grammatical errors, and ensuring a focused conclusion, you can make your essay more impactful and easier to follow. Remember, in writing, simplicity and clarity often yield the best results.

prompt: The youth pay more attention to entertainment celebrities than the historic figures of their nations
essay:
These days, the youth pay more attention to entertainment celebrities than the historic figures of their nations. Reasons behind this trend will be analyzed and several solutions should be proposed to improve the situation. 
 There are two reasons why young people are more attracted by famous music and movie stars instead of their countries' history. One reason is that social media gives wings to the spread of the fame of celebrities. It is common to notice that their work and live always grab the headlines of magazines, especially the online ones, drawing teenagers' attention toward these topics. Another reason is the tedious teaching method of history in school. Due to the lack of practical activities and learning by heart a great deal of information about historical events, students must experience feelings of boredom and stress. 
 However, solutions can be proposed to make the youth more intersted about the historical people of their countries. Firstly, movies may be an effective approach to bring history closer to teenagers. Moving images with sound will keep young people's fascinated about the sacrifice of national heroes and heroines in bloody wars, especially those which have their idols to star in. Secondly, the teaching method in history class can be tailored to arouse students' interests. For example, providing the students opportunities to visit museum or historical heritage can diversify their learning activities and thus promote their curiosity and memorization of historical names and events. 
 In conclusion, in spite of the neglect of young people for historic people of their countries and paying intention to famous celebrities, this trend can be improved by several suggested solutions.
feedback:
This essay presents an interesting perspective on why young people today might prioritize celebrities over historical figures, along with suggestions for how to address this issue. However, to enhance the effectiveness and clarity of the essay, there are several areas that can be improved.

Firstly, the essay successfully introduces the topic at hand but doesn't provide a clear roadmap of the discussion that will follow. A more detailed thesis statement could help guide the reader by explicitly stating the main reasons for this trend and the proposed solutions. This not only clarifies the purpose of the essay but also sets expectations for the reader.

Regarding coherence and cohesion, which refer to how well the essay flows and how ideas connect, there is room for improvement. To strengthen this aspect, begin each supporting paragraph with a clear topic sentence that states the main idea. For instance, when discussing reasons, start with the challenges related to presenting historical figures, followed by the influence of celebrities through social media. This structure helps the reader follow the analysis more naturally, providing a balance between problem and analysis before moving to solutions.

In terms of solutions, using a similar structured approach benefits the essay's coherence. Start with how historical movies can engage young people, then move on to interactive learning methods like museum visits. This order shows a logical progression from identifying issues to proposing well-thought-out solutions, enhancing the overall argumentative effectiveness of the essay.

On the language front, the essay is generally well-written, but there are occasional spelling and grammatical errors that could be refined. For instance, "interested" was misspelled as "intersted." Careful proofreading would ensure these small yet impactful mistakes are corrected, contributing to a polished final product.

The point of view is clear throughout the essay; however, explicit connections between the ideas would strengthen it further. Making sure each paragraph flows smoothly into the next, with connective words and phrases, can help reinforce the author's position and make the argument more persuasive.

Finally, specific examples or more descriptive details about how these strategies (like making historical movies or interactive learning) would work can enrich the essay, making the arguments more vivid and convincing to the reader.

In conclusion, by addressing these areas—clearly outlining the essay's structure, improving cohesion and coherence, correcting language mistakes, and providing more detailed examples—the essay could not only meet but surpass the basic requirements, achieving a higher level of proficiency and engagement with the reader.

prompt: Writing a summary on 2 news articles about obesity. The summary should be 100-300 words.
essay: The article "Obesity Can Trim 10 years Off Life" (2009) by Nanci Hellmich warns that obesity shortens life, and its effects rival those of smoking. Obesity causes heart disease and stroke which can reduce lifespan depending on whether an individual is overweight, obese or very obese; it ranges from a year for adults who are overweight to as much as 10 years for those who are very obese. The article presents a study reporting that a third of American adults are obese, and these same individuals are continually gaining weight. Finally, the article alerts that obesity is becoming a serious health concern and advises that we take actions now. \n Existing beliefs suggest that the obesity epidemic is caused by a combination of inactivity and excess calorie intake, but how much is each contributing to the problem? New research in the article "Too Many Calories Are to Blame For Obesity Surge, Not Lack of Exercise, Claim Scientists" (2009) by a Daily Mail reporter confidently suggests that excess calorie intake is more likely to be the cause of the rise in obesity than a lack of exercise. The study, conducted by Australian scientists at Deakin University, reveals that Americans may have been in fact increasing their amount of exercise over the past 30 years, yet the problem persists and expands. This implies that increasing physical activity as a solution is ineffective. The article concludes that existing and potential health policies should focus on reducing daily calorie intake.
feedback:
Your essay is a thoughtful exploration of the topic regarding the causes and consequences of obesity, referencing two different articles to support your argument. You've done a good job in presenting a clear point of view that obesity is a critical health issue, which is exacerbated more by excess calorie intake than by lack of exercise. This shows you understand the importance of addressing the topic and have engaged with it by presenting relevant information. Here are some points to help you improve your essay further:

1. **Response to the Topic**: Your essay responds to the topic by discussing the impact of obesity and exploring its causes. However, to strengthen your essay, consider including information on why the sources you've cited (the articles) are authoritative. For example, mention the study or research method used in each article to add credibility to the information you're presenting.

2. **Spelling, Grammar, or Stylistic Issues**: Your essay is generally well-written, but remember to keep an eye out for minor grammar mistakes and ensure consistent use of tenses throughout your essay. Also, try to vary your sentence structures to make your writing more engaging.

3. **Clarity of the Author's Point of View**: Your point of view is clear but could be made stronger by providing more depth to your argument. Consider discussing the implications of the studies you mention, and how they specifically support your view that reducing daily calorie intake should be a priority in health policies.

4. **Arguments and Descriptive Support**: While you've mentioned important findings from two articles, your essay would benefit from a more detailed explanation of these studies. For example, who conducted the research? Who participated in the studies? How were the studies carried out? This information will add depth to your argument and make your essay more persuasive.

Additionally, you might want to introduce the writers of the articles when possible, as it adds credibility and context. For the second article you referenced, try to include the name of the writer or the scientists involved in the study.

Lastly, remember that summarizing and analyzing articles requires careful attention to detail, ensuring that you accurately represent the authors' findings and arguments. Your essay is a solid starting point, but by addressing these points, you can make your argument stronger and more convincing.
